- `cmd/endless-event/main.go` — drop `migrate-db` subcommand; add `apply-change <path>` dispatcher
- `justfile` — `land` recipe gains a `BackupDB` + git-diff-driven `apply-change` loop between rebase and ff-merge
- `sqlc.yaml` — new
- `internal/queries/*.sql` — new (sqlc query files; minimal initial population)

## Risk: half-implementation

If sqlc adoption stalls mid-implementation, the framework removal could land with no replacement query system. Mitigation: sqlc is the LAST phase of this task. If work has to stop, the framework removal lands first (queries stay hand-written), sqlc becomes its own follow-up. The schema.sql + changes/ + apply-on-land model is independently usable.

## Verification

1. Existing tests pass after refactor: `go test ./internal/monitor/ -v`. The two old framework tests rewrite to drive schema.sql directly.
2. Fresh-DB init: rm `endless.db`, start endless, observe schema.sql creates everything, `_schema_version` is empty (no changes applied to a brand-new DB).
3. Existing-DB upgrade: backup current `endless.db`, run new binary on it, then run `just land` from a branch that has the E-1459 reshape change. Observe the 12 historical rows are backfilled to the TEXT-name shape with no data loss.
4. Apply-on-land: create a throwaway branch with a no-op change (`RegisterSQL("e-test", "SELECT 1;")`) in `internal/schema/changes/`, run `just land`, observe the change applies and a row appears in `_schema_version`.
5. Idempotency: re-run `just land` on the landed branch; the change is recognized as applied and skipped, no error.
6. sqlc: `sqlc generate` produces compilable Go code from the one representative query; the converted call site still passes its tests.

## Out of scope

- Migrating every hand-written SQL query to sqlc (one representative is enough to prove the pipeline; the rest is a follow-up task).
- Choosing a future production migration tool (atlas, golang-migrate, etc.) — that's "when we ship to users", not now.
- Removing `BackupDB()` from the codebase (kept as a callable utility per recommendation above).
- Renaming `_schema_version` table (the name still fits; changing it would inflate the diff for no clarity gain).

fields.prompt:
Implement E-1459 per the plan attached to the task. Read it with `endless task detail 1459` before doing anything.

This is a deliberate simplification: don't re-invent the abstractions the plan removes (no in-binary registry, no init() registrations, no RegisterSQL, no ApplyPending iteration). When in doubt, the simpler interpretation is correct.

Verification block is at the bottom of the plan. When tests pass and the reshape change applies cleanly on your real DB via `just land`, flip the task to `verify` with the verification steps copied into the outcome. Then close E-1380, E-1453, and E-1454 with "obsoleted by E-1459" outcomes.

payload.title: Add endless session next prospective remaining-work briefing
payload.description: A prospective, at-a-glance session view: Goal (task plus derived status facets like landed/assumed), a session-state verb, and a Remaining list split by next-actor — You (bare endless commands you run) and Agent (this session's own in-progress work). Derives from session_tasks plus task status plus phase; omits done-work, which stays in 'session activity'. Decomposes into a session_tasks.relation column, the read command, and the status-picker / plan-viewer verbs its output emits.

payload.title: Add session_tasks.relation column classifying how a task entered session scope
payload.description: Add a relation column to session_tasks classifying how each task entered this session's scope: goal (the claimed/active task), surfaced (created during the session), revisited (pre-existing, touched but not the goal). Set at capture time alongside the existing auto-capture. Feeds the You/Agent split and the Goal line in session next. Migration mechanism sequenced against E-1459, which is replacing the V-migration framework whose sequential numbering caused the V11 collision — avoid reintroducing it.

payload.title: Add endless task change interactive status-transition picker
payload.description: Add endless task change E-NNN: always presents an interactive picker of valid status transitions for the task (progression transitions first, then regressions), with no typed status value — selecting from the list is the point. Sugar over the existing status-update path. These are the commands session next emits in its Remaining list, so building them first makes that output runnable. Schema-free.

payload.title: Add endless task review/plan viewers and non-promoting draft-attach for plans
payload.description: Add endless task review E-NNN (render the task's plan text via glow -p) and endless task plan E-NNN (raw text to stdout). Add a draft-attach mode for plan text that does NOT auto-promote needs_plan to ready, so a drafted-but-unapproved plan is distinguishable in queries; approval is the explicit flip to ready and revision flips to revisit. Lets session next emit review-then-approve as runnable commands. Mostly schema-free.

payload.title: Implement endless session next briefing read command
payload.description: The read command that renders the briefing: Goal (task plus derived facets — landed from git-merge state, assumed/verify/etc. from status), a derived session-state verb (discussing/planning/implementing/awaiting-user/idle; stuck is awaiting-user with a blocker qualifier), and Remaining split into You (bare endless commands, ordered by phase, scoped to this session's verify/needs_plan-with-draft tasks) and Agent (this session's own in_progress work; auto-spawning the ready queue is out of scope, see E-1136). Bare commands, no prose prefixes. Derives from session_tasks(+relation), task status, and phase. Omits done-work.

payload.title: Split E-968 into focused sub-epics and reparent its children
payload.description: E-968 has accumulated 53 direct children spanning session data model, worktree infra, enforcement, plan-file handling, and more — too many to navigate and a source of noise. Triage them into a few focused sub-epics (e.g. session data model, worktree infrastructure, enforcement/gates), propose the regrouping for review, and reparent only after sign-off. Do NOT bulk-reparent without approval. Phase set to next; adjust if this should wait.

fields.outcome: Obsoleted by E-1459 which replaces the migrate.go framework with schema.sql + per-ticket scripts applied at land. The dry-run bug (monitor.DB() auto-migrates before -dry-run is consulted) lives entirely in the framework being removed; no in-place fix needed.

Obsoleted by E-1459 which replaces the migrate.go framework with schema.sql + per-ticket scripts applied at land. The RequiresRebuild gate is a Migration struct field in the framework being removed; the design problem (no fresh-DB escape for rebuild migrations) disappears with the framework. E-1462's description confirms E-1459 is motivated in part by avoiding sequential-version collisions like the V11/V10 race E-1396 hit.

Implemented and landed into main; reshape applied to the real DB. Verification (all pass): (1) go test ./internal/monitor plus kairos/events/sandbox suites pass; framework tests rewritten to drive schema.SQL directly. (2) Fresh DB: schema.sql creates all tables and _schema_version is empty (0 rows). (3) Real-DB reshape via endless db apply-change: 12 to 13 rows (12 vNN-slug names plus the e-1459-reshape-schema-version marker), new (name, applied_at) shape, data intact (tasks=856, sessions=512 unchanged before/after). (4) Dispatcher paths verified: normal .sql, .go runner (go run plus the runner helper), and bad-extension rejection. (5) Idempotency: re-running apply-change reports already applied (skipped); count stays 13. (6) Live toolchain reads the reshaped real DB. To re-verify: sqlite3 ~/.config/endless/endless.db "SELECT name FROM _schema_version ORDER BY name" lists the 13 names; PRAGMA table_info(_schema_version) shows name, applied_at. Bootstrap note: E-1459 own land used the safe sequence (land from main old justfile so no apply-change loop, then just build refreshes the global binary with apply-change, then apply-change against the real DB), because the global endless-event lacked apply-change until main was rebuilt; future lands run the loop automatically. sqlc adoption split to a follow-up task per decision.
Obsoleted by E-1459. The migrateV* functions and the entire migrate.go framework were deleted, so there are no error-swallowing migrateV* calls left to surface errors from.

Obsoleted by E-1459. The migrate-db command and its --dry-run flag, and the DB()-auto-migrate behavior, were all removed. DB() now only execs schema.SQL (no migration), so the dry-run-silently-applies bug cannot occur.
Obsoleted by E-1459. The RequiresRebuild framework gate was removed with migrate.go. Fresh DBs are now built directly from schema.SQL with no rebuild gate to break them.

payload.title: Adopt sqlc minimally now that schema.sql is the single source of truth
payload.description: Split out of E-1459. Add sqlc.yaml + internal/queries/ targeting internal/schema/schema.sql and convert one representative query (e.g. GetActiveTasks in internal/monitor/task.go) to generated code to prove the pipeline; leave the rest hand-written.

payload.title: Fix collision invalidation that marks live sessions ended on a bare pane-string match
payload.description: internal/monitor/session.go:271-283 collision invalidation sets state='ended' for every other sessions row sharing the incoming process (pane) string, with no liveness check. When two Claude session identities share one physical tmux pane (e.g. a worktree-keyed session and a main-checkout-keyed session during a land run from main), the live session is falsely marked ended; it only recovers on its next Stop hook (TouchSession upserts never restore state). Repro: E-1459 land run from the main checkout spawned queue-operation session a27e5710 on pane %203, flipping live session 510 to ended. Fix: verify the prior occupant is actually stale (tmux list-panes / last_activity recency / project-dir match) before invalidating.

payload.title: Rename 'prompt' to 'handoff' across CLI command, flag, and DB column
payload.description: The 'prompt' terminology causes drift: agents asked to write a 'prompt' tend to write plan content because 'prompt' semantically reads as 'design proposal.' Renaming to 'handoff' anchors the artifact as 'instructions for the spawned session about how to orient, communicate, and interact' — distinct from the plan (which is the design). Scope: (1) rename the 'endless task prompt' subcommand (src/endless/cli.py) to 'endless task handoff'; (2) rename the '--prompt' flag on 'task add' and 'task update' to '--handoff'; (3) rename the 'tasks.prompt' DB column to 'tasks.handoff' (sequenced against E-1459 which is replacing the migrate framework — coordinate so the rename uses whatever schema mechanism E-1459 lands); (4) update docs, CLAUDE.md, and any prompts/plans that reference the old names. Backward-compat aliases for a deprecation window are optional, not required (internal/dev tool, low caller count).

fields.title: Render handoff from template at spawn; drop tasks.prompt column and --prompt flag
fields.description: Eliminate per-task tasks.prompt content. Render the spawn handoff from a single template merged with task metadata (id, title) and runtime context (spawning pane id). The handoff is mostly boilerplate (orient, communication channel, default interaction rules, closing) — generating it removes the prompt-vs-plan drift entirely since agents no longer write prompts. Drops the tasks.prompt column, the --prompt flag on task add/update, and reshapes 'endless task prompt' into 'endless task handoff' which renders the template instead of displaying a stored field.

payload.title: Fix headless claude -p calls registering pane-owning sessions that false-end the caller
payload.description: Headless claude -p calls (verb-check at task_cmd.py:257; also recap) run subprocess.run with no env scrub, inheriting the caller TMUX_PANE. The headless call gets a new session UUID and fires a hook -> TouchSession(new_uuid, inherited_pane); the collision UPDATE at session.go:271-283 then sets state=ended on the CALLING live session (same pane, different uuid). Repro: a task add titled Adopt spawned the adopt verb-check and ended live session 510; while ended, the resolver (filters state!=ended) cannot resolve the caller, breaking endless session id and task add until the next Stop hook. Recap shares this (session_cmd.py:252 only hides such rows). Fix: scrub TMUX_PANE or set an internal marker in the claude -p env so endless-hook skips session registration+collision for headless calls; also harden the collision rule to confirm the prior pane occupant is actually stale before ending it.

fields.text:
# E-1470 implementation plan

## Root cause (confirmed in code + transcript)
Internal headless `claude -p` calls inherit the caller's `TMUX_PANE`. Each gets a fresh Claude
session UUID and fires `endless-hook` → `TouchSession(new_uuid, inherited_pane)`. The collision
`UPDATE` (`internal/monitor/session.go:271-283`) then sets `state='ended'` on every OTHER session
sharing that pane — i.e. the live CALLER. Repro: `endless task add` titled "Adopt…" spawned the
'adopt' verb-check (`_check_verb_via_haiku`, `src/endless/task_cmd.py:257`); its headless session
(`a27e5710`, transcript shows the "Is 'adopt' a verb?" prompt) ended live session 510. While
`ended`, the caller is invisible to the session resolver (filters `state != 'ended'`), breaking
`endless session id` / `endless task add` until the next Stop hook revives it.

## Fix
1. New env marker `ENDLESS_NO_HOOKS=true`. `endless-hook` (`cmd/endless-hook`) checks it first
   thing and exits 0 immediately — no `TouchSession`, no collision, no `SessionEnd` side effect.
   Hook-specific name (not a generic "internal" flag) so hook-suppression stays separable from
   any future internal-call concerns.
2. Route ALL internal `claude -p` calls through one helper (e.g. `_run_internal_claude(prompt,
   model)`) that runs:
       claude --model <m> -p <prompt> --effort low --tools "" \
              --mcp-config "" --strict-mcp-config --no-session-persistence
   with `env={**os.environ, "ENDLESS_NO_HOOKS": "true"}` (full env + marker — never a bare dict,
   which would strip PATH/auth and break `claude`).
   - `--tools ""` also removes PreToolUse/PostToolUse hook surface.
   - `--no-session-persistence` avoids littering `~/.claude/projects` with throwaway transcripts.
   - `--mcp-config "" --strict-mcp-config` spawns no MCP servers (Mike verified the empty-string
     parse is accepted).
   - `--effort low` keeps the trivial call cheap.
3. Convert both current internal call sites to the helper:
   - verb-check: `src/endless/task_cmd.py:257` (`_check_verb_via_haiku`).
   - recap: `src/endless/session_cmd.py:~728` (`claude -p`). Recap has the same latent bug —
     `session_cmd.py:252` only HIDES recap sessions from `session list`; once they stop
     registering at all, that band-aid filter can be retired.

## Considered and rejected
- `claude --bare`: skips hooks, but forces Anthropic auth to `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`/apiKeyHelper
  only and never reads OAuth/keychain. This environment has no API key (OAuth login; verified
  `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` unset), so `--bare` would fail to authenticate and silently break verb
  auto-registration. `ENDLESS_NO_HOOKS` preserves OAuth auth.
- `--session-id`: not a safety net (rejection accepted by Mike). If hooks fire (i.e.
  `ENDLESS_NO_HOOKS` failed), passing the caller's UUID means the headless call's `SessionEnd`
  hook → `EndSession(caller_uuid)` still ends the caller, just via a different path; a
  fresh/throwaway UUID just reproduces the collision. Only `ENDLESS_NO_HOOKS` closes both the
  collision and `SessionEnd` paths.

## Defense in depth (optional, can be its own task)
- Harden the collision rule (`session.go:271-283`) to confirm the prior pane occupant is actually
  stale before invalidating, rather than ending on a bare pane-string match.

## Related (split candidate)
- The CLI session resolver (`src/endless/task_cmd.py:1976-2017`) ignores `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`
  entirely; add a UUID-first authoritative lookup (`sessions.session_id` is UNIQUE), and fix that
  `ENDLESS_SESSION_ID` accepts only the integer PK while its error message implies any id (a UUID
  is silently ignored).

## Verification
- From a live Claude+tmux pane, run `endless task add` with a never-before-seen verb; confirm the
  caller session's `state` is unchanged (NOT flipped to `ended`) and `endless session id` still
  resolves it during and after the verb-check.
- Confirm no new transcript file appears under `~/.claude/projects/` for the verb-check.
- Same checks for `endless session recap`.

payload.title: Internal headless claude -p calls suppress hooks via ENDLESS_NO_HOOKS, not --bare or --session-id
payload.description: Verb-check (task_cmd.py:257) and recap shell out to headless claude -p. Unsuppressed, each inherits the caller TMUX_PANE, gets a fresh session UUID, and its hook trips the collision UPDATE (session.go:271-283) that false-ends the live caller. Fix: ENDLESS_NO_HOOKS=true so endless-hook self-suppresses (plus --tools '' --no-session-persistence --mcp-config '' --strict-mcp-config --effort low). Rejected --bare: forces ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/apiKeyHelper auth and never reads OAuth/keychain, which this env lacks, so it would fail auth and silently break verb auto-registration. Rejected --session-id: if hooks fire, SessionEnd on the caller UUID still ends the caller; a fresh UUID just re-collides. Only ENDLESS_NO_HOOKS closes both paths.

payload.title: schema.sql is the single source of truth; per-ticket change files applied at land replace the V-migration framework
payload.description: E-1459 deleted internal/monitor/migrate.go and the V-numbered framework. schema.sql now holds every table (CREATE IF NOT EXISTS, exec'd on each connection); destructive one-off changes live in internal/schema/changes/<e-NNN-slug>.{sql,go}, applied once at land via 'endless db apply-change', keyed by basename in a reshaped _schema_version(name, applied_at). No in-binary registry, no init() registration, no RegisterSQL, no ApplyPending. Rationale: pre-release/single-user, so the framework's rolling-upgrade payoff does not exist yet, but its costs do (V-number collisions across branches, per-change boilerplate, unfixed framework bugs E-1380/1453/1454 now obsoleted). sqlc adoption split to E-1467.

fields.text:
# Require explicit `--db main|worktree` for endless CLI inside self-dev worktrees

## Context — why this change

Endless is developed using endless. When a developer or an AI agent works inside a self-dev worktree, two distinct activities get conflated:

1. **Using endless to manage the project** — filing tasks, claiming, status updates, ledger entries. Belongs in the real DB (`~/.config/endless/endless.db`).
2. **Testing endless's own CLI behavior** — running commands to verify a code change. Must NOT pollute the real ledger; belongs in a throwaway per-worktree sandbox DB.

Today the choice is implicit: it depends on whether `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` was inherited from `.claude/settings.json` injection (→ sandbox) or not (→ real DB). Silent and mistake-prone — whoever isn't actively thinking about it writes to the wrong DB.

Concrete incident (2026-05-19): `endless task add "Testing bar"` run from a bare shell `cd`'d into `.endless/worktrees/e-1424/` landed in the **real ledger** as E-1425 — the developer believed it was going to the sandbox. Earlier incidents in the same vein: the E-1307 stale-session-id bug, the side-pane companion-discovery failure.

E-1281 established the original intent (isolate worktree DB writes from the real ledger). This task replaces implicit DB selection with an explicit, bypass-proof gate.

## The decision (locked with Mike, 2026-05-17/18/20)

- Manual `endless` CLI invoked with `cwd` inside `.endless/worktrees/e-*` of a project whose `.endless/config.json` has `worktree_sandbox: true` MUST pass `--db main|worktree`.
- If `--db` is absent in that context, refuse with an actionable error.
- **No env-var equivalent.** An env var can be exported once and silently defeat the gate for every later command — the exact silent-wrong-DB failure mode this task exists to kill. A per-invocation flag cannot be bypassed that way.
- `--db main` → operate on `~/.config/endless/endless.db`. `--db worktree` → operate on this worktree's per-worktree sandbox DB.
- Gate enforced at TWO layers:
  - **Python CLI layer** — parse `--db` on the root command; produce the human-facing refusal error.
  - **Go `DB()` layer** (`internal/monitor/db.go`) — refuse to open a connection when running in a self-dev worktree without an explicit context argument. Bypass-proof backstop: catches direct invocations of `endless-event` and any future Go binary that didn't get the flag wired through.
- Outside a self-dev worktree (main checkout, other projects, anywhere else) — no flag required, no gate, no behavior change.
- **`endless-hook` is exempt and hardcoded to the main DB.** It fires automatically on every Claude prompt; there is no human to type a flag. Hooks track real project state (sessions, activity, task bindings), not test artifacts — every Claude session is a real session regardless of which directory it works in. **Mechanism (locked 2026-05-20): the hook always passes `--db main` explicitly at every call site.** The Go `DB()` layer is not actor-aware — it sees the hook as a regular caller that happens to always pass `main`. Rationale: keeps `DB()` simple (one rule for all callers); makes the hook's main-DB decision locally visible in hook code rather than buried in a DB-layer special case; couples nothing across layers.
- No `is_dev_session` flag, no view-level filtering, no command-text introspection. Sessions are sessions.

## No allowlist — gate at DB access

An allowlist of subcommands exempt from the flag was considered and rejected (lists rot; new subcommands get forgotten). Instead the gate lives at `DB()`. Commands that never open the DB (`--help`, `--version`, `shell-init`, `endless session id` in pure-resolution mode) never hit the gate and work without `--db`. Coverage is automatic.

## Refusal message (locked 2026-05-20)

```
Error: running inside self-dev worktree requires an explicit --db value:

  --db main      the real ledger — managing the project
  --db worktree  this worktree's sandbox — testing endless itself

Need paths? Run `endless db path --db=main|worktree`.
```

Choices made and rationale (don't relitigate during planning):

- **No resolved DB paths in the error itself.** Use the `endless db path` companion command (see Sibling deliverable below).
- **No suggested default.** Newcomers should ask Claude which to use; defaulting risks pushing them toward the wrong choice.
- **No one-letter aliases yet** (`-m` / `-w`). Add only if typing becomes tedious in practice.
- **Don't echo the user's command in the error.** Click's usage line already covers it.

## Sibling deliverable: `endless db path` command

Add a small purpose-built print verb companion to the gate:

```
endless db path --db=main         # prints e.g. /Users/mike/.config/endless/endless.db
endless db path --db=worktree     # prints e.g. /Users/mike/.cache/endless/sandboxes/worktree-e-NNN/endless.db
```

`--db=worktree` resolves the sandbox dir from current worktree's cwd, the same way the gate resolves it. For users / agents who actually need the absolute path (SQL client debug, scripting). Referenced from the gate's refusal message so users have a one-command escape hatch.

**Must land WITH (or before) the gate**, so the error message's reference is functional immediately, not aspirational.

## Post-E-1426 model

E-1426 (urgent, assumed) retires companion JSON files, replacing them with idempotent `sessions.process` UPSERTs on every hook event. Write this task against that post-E-1426 world:
- Do NOT reference companion files as a DB-choice or discovery mechanism — they will not exist.
- Session identity lives in `sessions.process` in whichever DB the hook wrote to — which, given the hook is hardcoded to main, is always main.
- Sequencing: E-1426 is expected to land first. If it has not, coordinate so the hook's main-DB hardcoding does not fight E-1426's UPSERT path.

## Implementation shape

Three committable slices (any order across slices 1–2; slice 3 must land WITH or BEFORE the gate becomes user-facing):

1. **Go `DB()` gate** — detect self-dev-worktree cwd plus `worktree_sandbox: true` in the project config; require an explicit context argument; refuse otherwise. Tests.
2. **Python CLI `--db` flag** — root-level option; map `main` → real config dir, `worktree` → resolve the current worktree's sandbox dir; pass the resolved context to every Go subprocess as an explicit CLI argument (NOT an env var, to preserve the no-global-bypass property); emit the refusal error when missing. Tests.
3. **`endless db path` command** — see Sibling deliverable above. Small, isolated, lands with or before the gate.

## Sandbox-path resolution (locked 2026-05-21)

`--db worktree` resolves the sandbox dir from current worktree's cwd:

1. Walk up cwd to find `<project>/.endless/worktrees/<wt-dirname>/`.
2. Extract task_id from `<wt-dirname>` — reuse `monitor.TaskIDFromWorktreePath` on the Go side (already exists) and the equivalent on the Python side.
3. Build sandbox dir per current convention: `~/.cache/endless/sandboxes/worktree-e-{task_id}/`.

The current sandbox-naming convention is under review:
- **E-1446** — include the worktree slug in the sandbox dir name for 1-to-1 worktree↔sandbox mapping.
- **E-1447** — drop the redundant `worktree-` prefix.

If either lands before E-1429 implementation, update this task's resolution rule and verification step to match. If neither has landed, lock against the current convention.

## Coordination with E-1362 (snapshot file location move)

E-1362 moves `snapshotPlanFile`'s filesystem target from `<main-checkout>/.endless/plans/snapshots/` to `<main-checkout>/.git/info/endless/snapshots/`. The two tasks answer different questions:

- E-1429 decides **which checkout** the hook resolves to (always main, via the hook-uses-main rule).
- E-1362 decides **where within that checkout** the snapshot file lands.

They don't conflict, and either can land first. Either way, the hook's `ProjectPath()` lookup in `snapshotPlanFile` resolves to main checkout (because E-1429 makes hooks always use main, and E-1362's target is inside main anyway). The implementer of E-1429 must **not** touch `snapshotPlanFile`'s filesystem path — that change belongs to E-1362.

## Related tasks (NOT deliverables of this task)

- **E-1281** — this task `cleans_up` it; completes the original intent.
- **E-1330** — implements baseline `--mode seed` for sandbox init; complementary, not affected by this task.
- **E-1335** — adds user-configurable seed policy on top of E-1330; complementary, not affected by this task.
- **E-1414** — revised: precmd hook keeps PATH and `ENDLESS_SESSION_ID` duties, drops the `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` injection (superseded by `--db`).
- **E-1332** — revised: delayed sandbox reap instead of immediate destroy on worktree drop/land.
- **E-1426** — relates; see Post-E-1426 section.
- **E-1446** — sandbox naming change: include worktree slug. May alter the sandbox-path resolution rule above.
- **E-1447** — sandbox naming change: drop `worktree-` prefix. May alter the sandbox-path resolution rule above.
- **E-1337, E-1391** — substantively unaffected; their mechanics remain valid under the new gate.

## Verification

1. From main checkout: `endless task add "x"` works with no `--db` flag (no behavior change).
2. From inside `.endless/worktrees/e-NNN/`: `endless task add "x"` refuses with the exact refusal message above.
3. From inside the worktree: `endless --db main task add "x"` writes to the real ledger; `endless --db worktree task add "x"` writes to the sandbox DB. Verify each lands in the expected DB and NOT the other.
4. Direct Go binary invocation (`endless-event ...`) from inside a worktree with no context argument → Go `DB()` refuses.
5. `endless-hook` fired from inside a worktree → reads/writes the main DB, no refusal.
6. `endless --help`, `endless --version`, `endless shell-init` from inside a worktree → work without `--db` (never open the DB).
7. `endless db path --db=main` prints the real-DB path; `endless db path --db=worktree` from inside a worktree prints that worktree's sandbox-DB path.

# E-1469 — Render handoff from a template at spawn; drop `tasks.prompt`

## Context

Today a spawned session's opening prompt is **hand-authored per task** and stored in
`tasks.prompt`. Agents must write it, and because it sits right next to the plan
(`tasks.text`), the two drift — a prompt that contradicts the plan, or a plan
re-stated in the prompt. But the prompt is almost entirely boilerplate: orient,
read the guide + plan, default interaction rules,
return path, closing. The only per-task variables are the task id, title, and the
spawning pane.

This change deletes the stored field and **generates** the handoff from a single
template merged with task metadata + runtime context. Agents stop writing prompts
entirely, so prompt-vs-plan drift becomes structurally impossible. `tasks.text`
(the plan) remains the only authored, per-task narrative.

Decisions (confirmed with Mike this session):
- **Template lives in a standalone `.md` file**, loaded the same way `endless guide`
  loads `docs/guide/*.md` (a `__file__`-relative path; works because install is
  editable). No packaging work.
- **Lean template that delegates to `endless guide`.** Identity/origin + "read the
  guide + plan" + beacon + return path + brief closing. The drive-to-completion /
  loose-ends / tone rules stay in the guide as the single source.

Per the one-user / no-shipped-product rule, there is **no data migration** — existing
`tasks.prompt` content is throwaway and is dropped.

## The template

New file `docs/templates/handoff.md` (rendered with `string.Template`, `$field` syntax):

```
You're working on E-$spawned_id: $title.

You are creating a Markdown display component for the Endless web dashboard. Enter plan mode, explore the codebase, create a detailed plan, attach it as a subplan, get user approval, then implement.

## Context

The Endless dashboard displays plan items containing full markdown documents (headings, bullets, prose, code blocks). Currently rendered via goldmark Convert() producing raw HTML wrapped in prose-sm prose-invert Tailwind classes. The result is visually flat — headings have no margins or size differentiation, no interactivity.

Stack: Go + Templ + HTMX + Alpine.js + Tailwind CSS v4 + templUI components.

## Chosen Approach: AST to Templ Components

Parse markdown with goldmark to get the AST. Walk the tree and render each node as a templ component. This gives full control over styling and interactivity.

Goldmark exposes a full AST via parser.Parser.Parse() returning ast.Node. The tree can be walked with ast.Walk(). Each node type (heading, paragraph, list, code block) becomes a templ component.

## Requirements

- Headings are visually distinct (size, weight, color, margins above) and act as collapsible sections — clicking collapses everything until the next heading of same or higher level
- Paragraphs have proper spacing
- Bullet lists have proper indentation and markers
- Code blocks have background color, padding, ideally a copy button
- Inline code is visually distinct
- Bold/italic render correctly
- Component is reusable anywhere the dashboard displays markdown
- Short markdown (single paragraph, no headings) renders cleanly without collapse overhead

## Dismissed Alternatives

- CSS-only fix (style prose classes): Too limited, no collapsible sections or per-node control
- AST to styled HTML with Alpine.js injected: Mixes rendering concerns, hard to maintain
- Custom goldmark renderer: Outputs bytes not templ components, loses type safety

## Key Files to Explore

- internal/web/data/markdown.go — Current RenderMarkdown() to be replaced
- internal/web/pages/status_detail.templ — Primary consumer via templ.Raw(renderMarkdown())
- internal/web/pages/project_detail.templ — Also uses renderMarkdown()
- internal/web/pages/plan_detail.templ — Uses renderMarkdown()
- vendor/github.com/yuin/goldmark/ — Library with ast/ package
- vendor/github.com/templui/templui/components/collapsible/ — TemplUI collapsible
- vendor/github.com/templui/templui/components/accordion/ — TemplUI accordion

## Design Decisions for You

- Component API: single MarkdownContent(source string) component? Or parse externally and pass AST?
- templUI collapsible vs raw Alpine.js for heading collapse
- Expand all / collapse all controls
- Whether code blocks need syntax highlighting (probably not v1)
- Performance: parse on every render, or cache?

## Your Workflow

1. Enter plan mode
2. Explore the codebase — goldmark AST API, templUI collapsible, current rendering
3. Create a detailed implementation plan
4. Attach the plan: endless plan import <your-plan-file> --project endless --parent <this-item-id>
5. Get user approval
6. Exit plan mode and implement
7. When done: endless plan complete <this-item-id>

## Context

`snapshotPlanFile` currently swallows errors with `log.Printf` and returns `nil`. It's called from the `PostToolUse / Write` branch of the Claude hook to snapshot plan-file content into `.endless/plans/snapshots/`.

The fatal-vs-non-fatal criteria, established during E-989/E-1011 work:

**Non-fatal is justified ONLY when:**
1. The operation is opportunistic / best-effort by design (e.g., diagnostics, telemetry, snapshots that aren't required for correctness).
2. Failure is self-healing on a later event (e.g., `BackfillProcess` retries on the next `UserPromptSubmit`).
3. The error rate is high under normal operation and would be noisy if surfaced.

For comparison: `InitSession`, `SetProcess`, `WriteCompanion` are all fatal — silent failure means downstream features mysteriously break.

# E-971 Layer F — Task-command worktree integration

You are picking up the final E-971 slice (parallel-session support via git worktrees in endless). Read `.endless/plans/E-971.md` for full context. This prompt is the short brief.

## Context: what is already shipped

- **Foundation (commit `3954f9a`)**: `endless worktree {list, current, show, for-task}` — read-only inspection.
- **Mutation slice**: `endless worktree {land, drop}` with E-987's auto-commit + rebase + ff-merge + retry algorithm.
- **Layer D (commit `7199f5f`)**: SessionStart claim/adopt/create per-session worktree, PreToolUse worktree↔task match, SessionEnd lock release. Worktrees are now lifecycle-managed automatically.
- **Layer E (commit `c11ba0a`, E-1126)**: UserPromptSubmit handler with Layer 1 reminder + Layer 2 pivot gate. Pivot triggers pause Write/Edit until Claude clears via `task add`/`start`/`confirm`.
- **E-1117 + E-1124**: verbs were extracted from `config.json` into `.endless/verbs.json`. Treat verbs.json as the verb store, not config.json.
- **E-1138 (ready)**: separately covers updating land's auto-commit list (drop config.json, add verbs.json). NOT in your scope.

## Your scope: Layer F — task-command worktree integration

When `endless task start <id>` runs on a **plan-bearing task** (one whose `tasks.plan_file_path` is set per E-969), automatically create a dedicated **task worktree** if one doesn't already exist. This makes the per-task worktree lifecycle complete: per-session worktrees auto-create on SessionStart (Layer D); per-task worktrees auto-create on `task start` (this slice).

**Strictly this slice.** Do NOT modify SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PreToolUse, or worktree land/drop. Those are shipped.

### Behavior

In `src/endless/task_cmd.py`'s `start_item` (or wherever `task start` sets `status=in_progress` and binds the session to the task):

1. After the existing event emission for `task.started`, check if the task is **plan-bearing**:
   - `tasks.plan_file_path` is non-NULL AND the file exists at the endless-owned path `.endless/plans/E-NNN.md`.
2. If not plan-bearing: existing behavior, no worktree work.
3. If plan-bearing:
   - Compute branch name: `task/<id>-<slug>` where slug follows the rules in `.endless/plans/E-971.md` "Slug generation" section.
   - Compute worktree path: `<project_root>/.endless/worktrees/e-<id>` (matches the existing convention seen in e-967, e-1126, etc.).
   - Use `endless worktree for-task E-<id>` (or its underlying Python helper) to check if a worktree already exists. If yes: do nothing (the worktree is already there; subsequent SessionStart hooks will adopt it).
   - If no: invoke `git worktree add -b <branch> <path> main` (the existing land logic in `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py` already shells to git; reuse the same `_git_run` helper or factor a shared `add_worktree` function).
   - Write the companion file at `<worktree-root>/.endless/worktree.json`:
     ```json
     {
       "kind": "task",
       "task_id": "E-NNN",
       "base_branch": "main",
       "branch": "task/NNN-<slug>",
       "created_at": "<ISO timestamp>"
     }
     ```
     (No lock file — the lock comes from SessionStart when a session lands in the worktree, not from the task.start trigger.)
   - Print a cd-or-spawn hint to the user:
     ```
     Created task worktree: .endless/worktrees/e-NNN
     Switch sessions to it: cd .endless/worktrees/e-NNN
       or spawn a fresh one:    endless task spawn E-NNN
     ```

### Edge cases

- **Task starts but cwd is already in another worktree**: still create the dedicated task worktree; the user is informed via the hint and chooses whether to switch. Don't try to auto-cd; a child process can't change its parent shell's cwd.
- **Worktree creation fails** (e.g., branch already exists from an aborted attempt): surface the git error verbatim; don't silently retry. The user can `git worktree remove` and retry.
- **Plan file path stored but file missing**: treat as not-plan-bearing for safety. Add a warning to stderr.
- **Race with another session also starting the same task**: rare. The companion-file write should be atomic via `O_EXCL`. If the write loses the race, read the existing companion file and proceed as if you had created it.

### Slug computation

Implement (or reuse) the slug rule from the plan:
- Lowercase the title.
- Strip filler words: `a`, `an`, `the`, `to`, `from`, `of`, `for`, `with`, `in`, `on`, `at`, `by`, `and`, `or`.
- Replace non-alphanumeric with `-`.
- Collapse repeated `-`.
- Truncate to 40 chars at a word boundary.

Example: task E-757 "Move title verbs from hardcoded list to database table" → slug `move-title-verbs-hardcoded-list-database` → branch `task/757-move-title-verbs-hardcoded-list-database` → worktree `.endless/worktrees/e-757`.

Note: existing worktree directory naming convention (e.g., `.endless/worktrees/e-967`) uses just the task ID, NOT `<id>-<slug>`. The branch carries the slug; the directory does not. Match the existing convention.

## Acceptance criteria

- `endless task start E-NNN` on a non-plan-bearing task: no worktree created; existing behavior preserved.
- `endless task start E-NNN` on a plan-bearing task with no existing worktree: creates `.endless/worktrees/e-NNN/` on branch `task/NNN-<slug>` from main, writes the companion file, prints the cd-or-spawn hint.
- `endless task start E-NNN` on a plan-bearing task whose worktree already exists: no-op (no error, no duplicate creation), prints "worktree already exists at <path>".
- The created worktree is immediately discoverable via `endless worktree list` (state=active because companion file exists) and `endless worktree for-task E-NNN`.
- `endless worktree land E-NNN` on the created worktree successfully merges + cleans up (verifies the companion file shape is correct).

## Constraints and conventions

- **Companion file** terminology, not "sidecar".
- **Naming**: snake_case for code/DB identifiers, kebab-case for user-facing strings (CLI flags, branch names, slugs), Go uses PascalCase/camelCase.
- **Discipline**: do this work in a worktree. Layer D is now live, so SessionStart will create one for you if you start in main. Ship via `endless worktree land <task-id>`.
- **Use just install** from main only to refresh the toolchain after landing.
- **Don't run `just install` from your worktree**; use `PYTHONPATH=<worktree>/src endless ...` to test in-progress changes.
- **Don't modify** SessionStart hook, UserPromptSubmit hook, PreToolUse hook, or worktree land/drop logic. Those are shipped and out of your scope.
- Reuse existing helpers (`_git_run`, slug generators in worktree_cmd.py if any) rather than duplicating logic.

`.endless/plans/E-971.md`, particularly the "Worktree creation triggers" → "Task worktree" subsection and the "Slug generation" section.

Mark the task `verify` (Mike confirms). Use `endless worktree land <task-id>` to ship.

You are implementing **E-1307** — add a new subcommand `endless session id` that prints the current session's integer id to stdout for shell-substitution use.

## Why

E-1401 (just landed) added a gate in `emit_event` that refuses to fire when the resolver can't determine the session for a cli/hook actor. The gate's error message tells the user to do one of:

1. Run from a Claude pane.
2. Export `ENDLESS_SESSION_ID=<id>` (find ids via `endless session show`).
3. Export `ENDLESS_SESSION_ID="$(endless session id)"`.
4. Run `endless task bind <task-id>`.

Option 3 already references this command. Today it errors with "No such command 'id'". Shipping E-1307 makes option 3 live and lets us drop the verbose option 2.

## What to build

A new Click subcommand `endless session id` that:

- Takes no required arguments.
- Resolves the current session using the **same 3-layer logic** as `_current_endless_session_id` in `src/endless/task_cmd.py:1971` (env → pane-direct → single-sibling).
- On success: prints the integer EID on stdout, followed by a newline. **Nothing else on stdout** — no labels, no decoration. The output must be safe to interpolate as `ENDLESS_SESSION_ID="$(endless session id)"`.
- On failure (no session resolves): prints a one-line diagnostic to **stderr** explaining which layers were tried and exits non-zero.

**Reuse the existing resolver — don't reimplement it.** The cleanest path: import `_current_endless_session_id` from `task_cmd.py` (cross-module imports of leading-underscore functions are an established pattern in this codebase — `session_cmd.py` already calls into `task_cmd.py` and vice versa).

If the resolver returns None, you do need diagnostic detail for stderr — call `_find_sibling_claude_session` yourself to differentiate "no tmux" / "0 siblings" / "2+ siblings" / "no env var, no companion" cases, and tailor the stderr message accordingly. The user should know whether their problem is "not in tmux", "ambiguous siblings, claim from the right pane", or "no Claude session anywhere nearby".

## Files

- `src/endless/cli.py` — register the subcommand under `session_cmd` (follow the `cd` pattern at lines 610-629).
- `src/endless/session_cmd.py` — add the implementation function (e.g. `session_id_resolve()`); the `cd` resolver at line 1245 is the analogous reference.
- `src/endless/event_bridge.py` — after the new command is shipped, **delete the "Export ENDLESS_SESSION_ID=<id>; see `endless session show` for live session ids" hint** from the gate error message and remove the `# NOTE: once `endless session id` ships ...` comment block above it. The remaining options should become: (1) run from Claude pane, (2) the `$(endless session id)` form, (3) `endless task bind`.
- `tests/test_session_id.py` (new) — tests covering each resolution layer plus the failure path. Use the `no_session_stub` marker to opt out of conftest's autouse resolver stub (per E-1401).

## Test cases

1. `ENDLESS_SESSION_ID=42` env var → stdout is exactly `42\n`, exit 0.
2. `TMUX_PANE` matches a companion file → stdout is that companion's EID, exit 0.
3. No env, no TMUX_PANE → resolver fails → stderr names the cause, exit non-zero.
4. Exactly one sibling Claude pane → returns that EID.
5. Multiple sibling Claude panes → exit non-zero with stderr saying "ambiguous; claim from a specific pane".
6. The CLI invocation: `runner.invoke(main, ["session", "id"])` against each scenario above.

## Don'ts

- Don't print the UUID. ENDLESS_SESSION_ID must be the integer (the resolver in `_current_endless_session_id` only accepts digit strings).
- Don't print anything decorative on stdout. Bare integer + newline. Logs go to stderr if at all.
- Don't add `--format` / `--json` flags. One job, one output.
- Don't extend or modify `_current_endless_session_id` itself — its current behavior is load-bearing for E-1401's gate.

## E2E verification

From a Claude pane (where the resolver works):

```sh
endless session id
# Expect: bare integer, exit 0.

ENDLESS_SESSION_ID="$(endless session id)" endless task add "probe E-1307"
# Expect: task created, no E-1401 gate error.
```

From an unbound shell pane:

```sh
env -u ENDLESS_SESSION_ID -u TMUX_PANE endless session id

env -u ENDLESS_SESSION_ID -u TMUX_PANE -- bash -c 'echo "[$(endless session id 2>/dev/null)]"'

While that's unfixed, the **pane-direct layer** and **sibling layer** can both fail for sessions started in affected environments. That's E-1408's problem to solve. Your job is to surface the existing resolver's verdict cleanly — don't try to compensate.

You are working on **E-1323** (Add smarter dedup for session_statuses to catch agent-retry near-duplicates within a short window).

## What the bug looks like

Current dedup in `internal/events/session_status.go:isDuplicateOfLatest` is strict byte-equal on every content column against the latest row for the session. This catches the literal "called add twice with the exact same XML" case, but misses the more common case in practice: an agent retries the same status with slight rewording (em-dash replaced, sentence trimmed, an apostrophe escaped differently) and produces two rows the user perceives as identical but the byte-equal check sees as different.

Concrete incident in session 389:

- Row id=7 inserted at `2026-05-14T20:52:48` from a first attempt that the user interrupted.
- Row id=8 inserted at `2026-05-14T20:54:48` from the agent's retry after "Continue."
- The two rows differed by ~169 bytes total (87 in `decisions`, 82 in `summary`) — the agent rewrote sentences during the retry.
- User considered them duplicates; manually deleted row 8 as cleanup.

Strict byte-equal worked as designed; the design was too narrow for the usage pattern that emerged.

## 2. Do the work

Enter plan mode.

Three direction options to discuss with Mike before implementing:

**A. Time-window-based skip.** If any row exists for this session_id within the last N seconds (configurable, default maybe 60s), refuse the insert with a clear error message: "near-duplicate window in effect; previous status was recorded N seconds ago. Use --force to insert anyway." Cheap; doesn't need similarity computation; gives the agent explicit feedback.

**B. Semantic similarity threshold.** Compute a hash of normalized content (strip whitespace, normalize punctuation) or a Levenshtein/Jaccard score against the previous row. Skip-or-refuse if above threshold. More accurate but more code, and "what's the right threshold" is a tuning question.

**C. Combine: window + similarity.** Within N seconds, require similarity below threshold; outside the window, allow. Belt-and-suspenders.

**D. The "add --amend" approach.** Instead of refusing the second add, treat it as an amendment to the previous row — copy the previous row's content, apply the delta, INSERT a new row that's the merged version. Requires a clear semantics for "what counts as the merged version" per section. Heavier than (A), but handles the case where the agent IS legitimately updating the session's reported state.

Mike's earlier framing favored an explicit `--amend` flow eventually, but that's a bigger lift. **Recommendation for v1: option A (time-window skip) with `--force` escape hatch.** Simplest; clear feedback; doesn't preclude (D) later.

Open design questions for plan-mode chat:

- Default window size — 30 seconds? 60? 5 minutes? Mike's intuition will inform this.
- The error message: what does the agent need to know? (Window remaining, last row id, command to override.)

You are working on **E-1324** (Fix `endless session status add` hang after successful insert).

## What the bug looks like

`endless session status add` is invoked with valid XML. The Go-side flow completes successfully — the row lands in `session_statuses`, child rows in `session_status_tasks`, the markdown render is computed and (in observed cases) ALREADY flushed to the calling shell's stdout buffer. But the CLI process does not terminate. From the calling shell's perspective the command appears hung; in Claude Code's Bash tool this shows as "Channeling..." for 1–2+ minutes until a manual interrupt.

Three concrete observed incidents in session 389:

- First reproduction: ~2m 15s before manual interrupt. Row inserted at `2026-05-14T20:52:48`; output already in stdout buffer showing the full markdown render including "## Notes" / "Four dogfood misses..." (visible in the Bash tool output, 46 lines / 1m 56s elapsed).
- Second reproduction: identical symptom after retrying the same `add` invocation with slightly reworded XML (the rewording is incidental — the hang reproduces independently). Inserted at `2026-05-14T20:54:48`, hung until manual interrupt.
- Reproduction at file-time of this task: same shape, same hang. The bug is consistent.

The INSERT succeeded each time. Verify with:

```
endless sql "SELECT id, created_at FROM session_statuses WHERE session_id = 389 ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 5"
```

## What's likely happening (your starting hypothesis to validate or refute)

`src/endless/event_bridge.py:emit_event` calls `subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True)`. `capture_output=True` blocks until BOTH the subprocess exits AND its stdout/stderr pipes drain to EOF. If the child process holds an inherited file descriptor open past its main goroutine's return, the pipe never closes, and `run()` blocks forever (well, until the parent dies or interrupts).

Candidate fds that could leak in `endless-event`:

- `*sql.DB` connection pool — if `Execute()` returns without explicitly closing, the pool's keep-open connection might keep the process from exiting. Go's `database/sql` should clean up on `main()` exit but sqlite drivers sometimes have shutdown quirks.
- Stdout/stderr buffers that aren't explicitly `Sync()`'d before `os.Exit()`.
- An inherited fd from `endless-event`'s own subprocess (it shells to `git` for the ledger commit — `internal/events/commit.go` `CommitLedgerSegment` uses `exec.Command.CombinedOutput`; if THAT subprocess leaks fds back to the parent, the parent's stdout pipe could stay alive).

The third hypothesis is the most likely. `CombinedOutput()` reads stdout+stderr fully and waits for the git subprocess to exit. But if git inherits the parent's stdout fd and doesn't close it before exiting, that fd remains alive in the parent. The Python `subprocess.run(..., capture_output=True)` is reading from the parent's stdout pipe; the pipe's write end is still held by... someone (the git fd in the worst case, or a stray Go runtime fd).

Diagnostic steps before designing the fix:

1. **Reproduce with the simplest possible XML.** A `<session-status><headline>test</headline></session-status>` payload. If the hang reproduces, the bug is independent of payload complexity.
2. **Reproduce outside Claude Code's Bash tool.** Run from a vanilla shell. If it hangs there too, the bug is in endless-event, not in Claude Code's tool integration.
3. **Strace/dtrace the endless-event process** at hang time:
   ```
   sudo dtrace -p $(pgrep endless-event) -n 'syscall:::entry { ustack(); }'
   ```
   or use `lsof -p <pid>` to see what fds are open.
4. **py-spy or strace the Python parent** to see where `subprocess.run` is blocked:
   ```

   ```
5. **Check if endless-event terminates AT ALL** — `ps` for the pid; does the process actually exit, or is it sitting in some final stage?

Once the cause is isolated, candidate fixes (don't apply until you've validated the root cause):

- **Explicit `os.Exit(0)` at end of `runEmit`** — forces process termination regardless of background goroutines. Cheap; effective. May lose deferred cleanup.
- **Close stdout/stderr explicitly** before return in Go (`os.Stdout.Sync()` then `os.Stdout.Close()`).
- **Switch Python `subprocess.run` to `Popen` + read-then-wait with timeout.** Drain stdout/stderr explicitly, then wait. Bound the wait at a few seconds; if exceeded, kill the subprocess.
- **Explicitly close `*sql.DB` in `Execute()`'s caller** before returning from `endless-event`. Sqlite drivers can hold a daemon goroutine on the connection pool.
- **Pass `exec.Command.Stdin = nil` AND explicit nil for stdout/stderr** to git subprocesses so they can't inherit fds back from the parent. Worth trying as a no-cost defensive measure.

You are working on **E-1327** (Decide session-status data model: snapshot+slots vs event-stream+composition).

**This is a `decision`-type task.** Deliverable is a written design conclusion with rationale, captured in the task's text and outcome — not code. Subsequent implementation tasks will reference this decision as their architectural premise.

**Constraint: this decision precedes E-1319.** The read CLI (`endless session status list/show/latest`) cannot meaningfully be designed until the data model is defined. Don't let E-1319 unblock without an answer here.

## What surfaced the question

The current `session_statuses` table treats each row as a point-in-time snapshot of a session's reported state. The docs (`endless guide sessions`, line "Latest row by `created_at` is the current status") promise snapshot semantics. But in actual usage:

- Agents (Claude sessions) record DELTAS — what just changed in the recent turns — because re-stating everything every time is token-expensive and bog-slow. The latest row never contains the full session view.
- Readers expect snapshots and don't get them. The docs lie.
- Multi-phase sessions get flattened: only the most-recent phase shows; earlier arcs disappear from "current state."

So the model and the behavior are misaligned.

- **Gap #5:** empty sections clutter render

Plus two renderer wins (#6: auto-join task titles to IDs; #7: `<to-file>` slot for observation-to-task conversion).

```

### 4. The conversation history that produced this task

This decision task was created at the tail end of a long ES-389 conversation. The exchanges leading up to its filing covered:

- The dogfood-reflex problem (5 misses in one session) and why event-stream auto-capture would lower the recording cost
- Mike's filing-vs-activity distinction (filings idempotent; activity is time-series)
- The retry-rewording dup-row incident that led to E-1323
- The CLI hang that led to E-1324
- The observation that the docs and behavior are misaligned (no row is a true snapshot)

Read the latest few session_statuses rows (id=4, id=5, id=7) for the trail.

## The actual decision

Three named options:

**(A) Snapshot+slots.** Keep the per-row snapshot model. Add structured slots for what's currently missing: `<test-probes>`, `<handoff>`, `<runbook>`, `<to-file>`. Improve the renderer (suppress empty sections, auto-join task titles). Each row is still meant to be a complete snapshot of session state at a moment. The dedup model stays per-row.

Pros: incremental; rows remain self-contained; the read model stays "fetch the latest row." Cons: the underlying token-cost / re-statement problem is unsolved; agents will keep recording deltas, and the snapshot promise will keep breaking.

**(B) Event-stream + composition.** Each aspect (tasks, decisions, commits, memory, summary, notes, headline, runbooks, handoffs) becomes its own table. Some are auto-captured as side effects of existing events (E-1322 is the first slice for task filings). Some are appended by `endless session status add` calls. There is no parent "row" — there's a session, and there's a stream of typed events. "Current state" is composed by JOINs/aggregations in the read CLI.

Pros: matches how agents actually record (deltas append); auto-capture reduces dogfood-reflex cost; rich queryability; no flattening of multi-phase sessions. Cons: bigger schema lift; reads become aggregations not row fetches; the "snapshot as a thing" goes away.

**(C) Hybrid.** Keep `session_statuses` rows for *narrative* aspects that are intrinsically per-recording (headline, summary, notes, decisions). Add per-aspect child tables for *cumulative* aspects (tasks, commits, memory, handoffs, runbooks). Reads compose: "latest narrative + cumulative items since session start" or similar.

Pros: pragmatic; preserves familiar row-fetch reads for narrative; aligns event-stream where it adds value. Cons: two models to maintain; "snapshot vs event" decision pushed down to per-aspect basis.

# E-1337: Stop deleting worktrees at land; record landings in DB; reap by sweep

## Problem

`endless worktree land` calls `git worktree remove` (see the land flow in `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py`), which deletes the worktree directory. This breaks the **currently-running Claude session** whose cwd is the worktree (the dir disappears underneath it; the active session, not just future `claude --resume` attempts, is what breaks). Hit during E-1281's land — the dir had to be recovered from IntelliJ local history. Suspected related to E-1372's "SessionStart hook did not re-register after worktree-delete-on-land" symptom.

## Approach

**Don't delete anything at land time.** The cleanup at the end of land was incidental and is what introduced the bug.

Land flow becomes:
1. Rebase the branch onto main.
2. ff-merge into main.
3. `INSERT INTO task_landings(...)` recording the event.
4. (Branch stays. Worktree dir stays. Nothing is removed.)

Re-landing (when a bug is found post-land in the same task) is the same flow: new commit on the branch, rebase, ff-merge, INSERT another row. `task_landings` is append-only history.

## Schema addition

```sql
CREATE TABLE task_landings (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  task_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(id),
  session_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES sessions(id),
  branch TEXT NOT NULL,
  merge_commit_sha TEXT NOT NULL,
  landed_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL
);
```

Migration follows existing endless DB migration patterns.

## Reaper sweep

A sweep that periodically removes stale worktrees that no live process is using.

**Detection logic** (per dir in `.endless/worktrees/*`):
1. Get `task_id` from the dir name (`e-NNNN` → `E-NNNN`).
2. `SELECT MAX(landed_at) FROM task_landings WHERE task_id = ?`
   - No rows → in-progress task, not landed yet → skip.
3. If `MAX(landed_at) > config.worktree_ttl ago` AND `lsof -d cwd +D <dir>` shows no live process with cwd in the dir → eligible to reap.

**Reap action:**
- `git worktree remove --force <dir>` (the supported git command — handles `.git/worktrees/<id>/` cleanup)
- `git branch -D <branch>` (branch name from the latest `task_landings` row)
- Nothing else (no marker file to clean up; no archive to remove)

**Pre-existing orphan dirs** (the ~10 currently in `.endless/worktrees/` from before this work, with no entries in `task_landings`) are skipped — the reaper only touches dirs whose task has at least one landing record.

## Configuration

Add `worktree_ttl` to `.endless/config.json` project config. Default: 14 days. Format: duration string (`"14d"`, `"24h"`, etc.) or integer seconds — implementer's call, document the choice.

## Sweep trigger points

Sweep runs (cheaply) at these invocations rather than as a background daemon:
- `endless-hook` firings (PostToolUse, PreToolUse, etc.) — picks up the routine cases
- `endless task claim`
- `endless task spawn`
- `endless worktree land`

Daemon is a future concern, not in scope here.

## lsof caveat

`lsof -d cwd +D <dir>` is fast enough for the current worktree count (~25 dirs). It can emit warnings on stat() failures for unrelated filesystems, e.g.:

```
lsof: WARNING: can't stat() smbfs file system /Volumes/.timemachine/...
    Output information may be incomplete.
    assuming "dev=36001a9b" from mount table
```

These warnings are noise for our use case. Filter known warning patterns from stderr (or `2>/dev/null`); rely on the exit code and stdout to determine whether the dir has live processes. Do not let warnings about unrelated paths affect the reap decision.

## E-1426 has landed

E-1426 (companion-file elimination, sessions.process UPSERTs on every hook) is in main. The reaper's process-detection can rely on `lsof` directly without needing to inspect any companion files. No interaction with companion-file plumbing needed.

## Related and downstream

- **E-1372** (`SessionStart hook did not re-register Claude session after worktree-delete-on-land`): downstream of this root cause. Once this lands, run E-1372's repro to confirm whether the SessionStart-on-reincarnate gap resolves structurally or whether a separate fix remains.
- **E-1281**: the task whose land first surfaced the bug.
- **E-1421** (curated 'next' list): blocked on this task; unblocks once this lands.
- **Post-MVP — undo**: a future feature that reverses a land. The "don't delete anything" approach in this task makes undo feasible (the branch, worktree, and tip refs are all still there). Undo does NOT delete `task_landings` rows — that's append-only history; undo would add a separate record (design TBD).

## Verification

1. From a worktree with an active Claude session (cwd = worktree path):
   - Run `endless worktree land <id>`.
   - Land succeeds; the dir still exists; the branch still exists; `git worktree list` still shows it; a new row appears in `task_landings`.
   - The active Claude session continues without disruption — `pwd` still resolves; subsequent commands work; no SessionEnd fires.

2. Re-land scenario:
   - In the same worktree, make a new commit (a bug fix).
   - `endless worktree land <id>` again.
   - Second row appears in `task_landings`; first row preserved.

3. Reaper sweep:
   - Worktree with `MAX(landed_at)` younger than `worktree_ttl` → does NOT reap.
   - Advance time (or lower `worktree_ttl` for the test) → reaper runs at next trigger, reaps successfully (worktree removed, branch deleted).
   - Worktree with `MAX(landed_at)` older than `worktree_ttl` but a process has cwd inside → does NOT reap.
   - Pre-existing orphan dir (no rows in `task_landings`) → does NOT reap; logged for awareness only.

4. lsof stderr filter:
   - Run the reaper on a machine with dead mount-table entries; warnings appear in lsof stderr but do not affect the reap decision.

## Instructions for the implementing session

1. Read this prompt + `endless guide` + relevant code (`src/endless/worktree_cmd.py`'s `land` flow).
2. Write the plan file at `.endless/plans/E-1337.md` covering: schema migration, code touchpoints in worktree_cmd.py, reaper module location, sweep trigger wiring, config schema addition, test strategy.
3. Attach via `endless task update E-1337 --text .endless/plans/E-1337.md` (auto-promotes to `ready`).
4. Implement. Tests required for: land happy path, re-land, reaper grace-period boundary, reaper live-process protection, lsof warning tolerance.
5. Flip to `verify` when done, including the verification block above in the handoff message.

# Move `snapshotPlanFile` target to `.git/info/endless/snapshots/{pending,e-NNN}/`

## Context — E-1361's decision

E-1361 (confirmed) decided: verbs.json and plan snapshots are stored as plain files in `<main-checkout>/.git/info/endless/`, shared across the project's worktrees, and reach project main only via worktree land. Snapshots are immutable per-task entries; verbs.json is a single mutable file.

For snapshots specifically: pending entries (no task bound to the session yet) live in `<main>/.git/info/endless/snapshots/pending/`. When a task is claimed against the session, pending entries belonging to that session move into `<main>/.git/info/endless/snapshots/e-NNN/`. At worktree land, E-1364 moves the `e-NNN/` contents into the worktree branch's `.endless/plans/snapshots/` so they reach project main.

This task implements that decision for the snapshot side. E-1363 implements it for verbs.json. E-1364 ties them into worktree land.

## What changes

### 1. New write target

`snapshotPlanFile` in `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go:898` today writes to `<project_root>/.endless/plans/snapshots/<ts>-<sha8>.{md,json}`, where `<project_root>` comes from `monitor.ProjectPath(projectID)` (DB lookup).

New target:
- `<main-checkout>/.git/info/endless/snapshots/pending/<ts>-<sha8>.{md,json}` when no task is bound to the session.
- `<main-checkout>/.git/info/endless/snapshots/e-NNN/<ts>-<sha8>.{md,json}` when the session has an active_task_id.

Existing filename convention (`<ts>-<sha8>`) preserved.

### 2. Resolve main checkout via git, not `ProjectPath`

**Critical:** do NOT resolve the new target via `monitor.ProjectPath()`. That call goes through the DB; under the pre-E-1429 implicit-DB-choice world it can return the worktree checkout (when the hook's `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` points at a sandbox whose `projects` table has the worktree as the registered project, via `ensureAutoRegisteredProject`'s auto-insert behavior) instead of the main checkout. E-1361's decision says snapshots are *always* in main checkout's `.git/info/`, regardless of DB context — so use git itself to find the main checkout, not endless's project registry.

Use git directly:
```
git -C <cwd> rev-parse --git-common-dir
```
returns the main `.git/` directory regardless of which worktree the call is made from. The snapshot target is `<git-common-dir>/info/endless/snapshots/`.

This decouples the snapshot path entirely from the DB-choice question — E-1429 doesn't affect this task and this task doesn't depend on E-1429.

### 3. Sweep at task claim

When a session claims a task (`endless task claim E-NNN` or `endless task bind`):
- Scan `<main>/.git/info/endless/snapshots/pending/` for entries owned by this session.
- Move them to `<main>/.git/info/endless/snapshots/e-NNN/`.
- The "owned by this session" check: each snapshot's companion-style metadata (filename or in-file) needs a session_id. Decide during planning: filename embeds session UUID, or each `.json` file carries it, or a sidecar index. E-1361's outcome says "session_id in companion file" for pending entries — confirm what that means concretely in the planning step.

Open question: is the sweep idempotent (safe to run more than once if claim flow re-fires)? Should be yes.

### 4. Remove `IsSandboxActive()` gating

`snapshotPlanFile` has an `IsSandboxActive()` check (added by E-1354) that skips snapshot commits in sandboxed sessions. Once snapshots no longer commit at write time (they're written, not committed; E-1364 commits them via worktree land), the sandbox-skip is dead code. Remove the call from `snapshotPlanFile`.

The `IsSandboxActive()` helper itself can stay (may be useful elsewhere) or be removed if no other caller exists. Decide during planning based on cross-reference search.

### 5. Migration of existing snapshots

Today's snapshots already exist under `<project>/.endless/plans/snapshots/` across multiple worktrees and the main checkout. Decide during planning:
- Leave them in place; new snapshots go to the new target only. Old ones never reach main via the new land mechanism but were presumably already committed via the old AUTO_COMMIT_GLOBS path.
- Or migrate (move/copy them into `.git/info/endless/snapshots/` retroactively).

My lean: leave in place. Old snapshots are already in their respective worktrees; the new mechanism starts fresh. Confirm with Mike.

## Coordination

- **E-1429 (require explicit `--db`)** — E-1429's hook-uses-main rule would also make `ProjectPath` resolve to main, but we don't rely on that; we use `git rev-parse --git-common-dir` instead. The two tasks don't conflict and either can land first. E-1429's prompt has a corresponding note.
- **E-1363 (move verbs.json)** — sibling implementation child of E-1361. Independent; can land in either order.
- **E-1364 (worktree land bundles snapshots)** — blocked-by this task. Once snapshots live in `.git/info/endless/snapshots/e-NNN/`, E-1364 moves them into the worktree branch at land time so they reach project main.

## Open design items — present to Mike, do not decide alone

1. **Session-id ownership of pending entries.** How is "this pending snapshot belongs to this session" encoded? Filename suffix (`<ts>-<sha8>-<session-uuid>.json`), in-file `session_id` field, or sidecar index? E-1361's outcome says "companion file" — clarify the concrete representation.
2. **Migration of existing `.endless/plans/snapshots/`.** Leave in place vs. retroactively migrate. My lean: leave in place.
3. **Removal of `IsSandboxActive()` helper.** Remove entirely vs. keep for future callers. Decide after cross-reference search.

## Verification

1. From a Claude session: edit and exit a plan via `ExitPlanMode`. Snapshot lands at `<main-checkout>/.git/info/endless/snapshots/pending/<ts>-<sha8>.{md,json}`.
2. Same session then claims a task (`endless task claim E-NNN`). The pending snapshot is moved to `<main-checkout>/.git/info/endless/snapshots/e-NNN/`.
3. From a Claude session in a sandboxed worktree: snapshot also lands at `<main-checkout>/.git/info/endless/snapshots/...` — NOT at `<worktree>/.git/info/...` (confirm `git rev-parse --git-common-dir` correctly returns the shared `.git/`).
4. `IsSandboxActive()` call removed from `snapshotPlanFile`; tests still pass.
5. Subsequent `endless worktree land E-NNN` (E-1364) moves the snapshots from `.git/info/endless/snapshots/e-NNN/` to the worktree's `.endless/plans/snapshots/` and bundles them into project main (verified once E-1364 lands).

You are implementing **E-1381** — fix the auto-capture guard in `session_tasks` so it actually records rows from real `endless task add` / `endless task update` / `endless task claim` etc. invocations. E-1322 landed (commit `4652b1a` on main) but its guard is incoherent with the actual actor model; no rows have ever been produced in production. The fix is one line of code plus updating tests that asserted the buggy behavior.

## The bug

`internal/events/session_tasks.go` defines:

```go
func shouldRecordSessionTouch(evt *Event) bool {
    return evt.Actor.Kind == ActorSession && evt.Actor.SessionID != ""
}
```

But `Actor.Kind` describes event ORIGIN (`cli` / `hook` / `web` / `system`) per its docstring in `internal/events/event.go`, not author. The ledger evidence:

```
$ grep -ho '"actor":{"kind":"[a-z]*"' .endless/db-ledger/*.jsonl | sort | uniq -c
1518 "actor":{"kind":"cli"
```

Zero events ever emitted with `kind=session`. Every real `endless task add` carries `kind=cli` with `session_id` populated from TMUX_PANE resolution. The current guard rejects all of them.

## The fix

In `internal/events/session_tasks.go`, change the guard to:

```go
func shouldRecordSessionTouch(evt *Event) bool {
    return evt.Actor.SessionID != ""
}
```

Drop the `ActorKind` check entirely. SessionID alone is the criterion: if the event carries session attribution, record the touch; if not, skip.

Update the docstring on `shouldRecordSessionTouch` to reflect the corrected semantics. The previous comment ("ActorKind == ActorSession only, even though CLI/hook actors may carry a session_id") is wrong and should be removed.

## Tests to fix

In `internal/events/session_tasks_test.go`:

- **`TestSessionTasks_NoRowForCLIActor`** asserts the buggy behavior (CLI actor with session_id produces no row). Either:
  - Rename it to `TestSessionTasks_CLIActorWithSessionRecordsRow` and assert the opposite (one row, populated from SessionID), OR
  - Delete it and add a new positive case for CLI-actor-with-session.
  - Add another negative case: CLI actor with EMPTY session_id should NOT produce a row. The "guard rejects when SessionID is empty" path still needs coverage.

- **`TestSessionTasks_NoRowForEmptySessionID`** stays — empty SessionID still skips the upsert.

- The existing positive tests (`TestSessionTasks_CreatedBySession`, etc.) probably use a hand-built `ActorSession` kind actor. Adjust them to use `kind=cli` with a `session_id` set, since that's the realistic shape. The test should mirror production.

1. `just build` cleanly.
2. `just test` passes.

   ```

You are implementing **E-1391** — worktree lifecycle visibility. Three pieces in one slice: new event kinds, new materialized table, new read CLI. Implements E-1352 (events-as-primitive); follows E-1322's pattern (commit `4652b1a` on main).

## What this is

Today, `endless worktree create` / `endless worktree drop` (and the underlying `endless task claim` auto-create path) modify the filesystem but emit no DB-recorded events. There's no answer to "which worktrees does session N have, and which did it once have?" except by scanning the filesystem.

Three pieces:

1. **Two new event kinds**: `worktree.created`, `worktree.destroyed`. Emitted from the worktree create/drop paths in the endless CLI.
2. **New table** `session_worktrees(session_id, worktree_path, branch_name, created_at, destroyed_at NULL until destroy)`. UNIQUE(session_id, worktree_path). Auto-captured via handlers.
3. **New CLI** `endless session worktrees [--session N] [--active]` listing active and historical worktrees for a session.

## Schema (V10 migration in `internal/monitor/db.go`)

```sql
CREATE TABLE session_worktrees (
    id             INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    session_id     INTEGER NOT NULL,
    worktree_path  TEXT    NOT NULL,
    branch_name    TEXT    NOT NULL,
    created_at     TEXT    NOT NULL,
    destroyed_at   TEXT    NULL,
    UNIQUE(session_id, worktree_path)
);
CREATE INDEX idx_session_worktrees_active ON session_worktrees(session_id, destroyed_at) WHERE destroyed_at IS NULL;
```

**ALL tables get a surrogate `id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY` as the first column** — Mike's 35+-year DB rule, no exceptions. The UNIQUE(session_id, worktree_path) stays as a constraint but is NOT the primary key. E-1322's session_tasks landed without an id PK; that's being fixed in E-1396. Do not repeat the omission here.

NO FK on session_id (matches the rationale on session_tasks per `4652b1a`: a materialized index must outlive its referenced entities; the JSONL ledger is the audit source).

Add two new Kind constants:

KindWorktreeDestroyed Kind = "worktree.destroyed"
```

Register both in `ValidKinds`. Payloads in `internal/events/payload.go`:

```go

    Path        string `json:"path"`
    BranchName  string `json:"branch_name"`
    TaskID      int64  `json:"task_id,omitempty"`
}
type WorktreeDestroyedPayload struct {
    Path string `json:"path"`
}
```

## Handlers (`internal/events/executor.go`)

Two new exec funcs: `execWorktreeCreated`, `execWorktreeDestroyed`. Wire to dispatch switch. Both check `shouldRecordSessionTouch(evt)` — **use the same helper that E-1381 fixed; the guard is `evt.Actor.SessionID != ""`, NOT `Kind == ActorSession`**. If E-1381 isn't merged yet, do the fix as part of this task — but verify it landed separately first.

execWorktreeCreated: INSERT into session_worktrees (session_id, worktree_path, branch_name, created_at).
execWorktreeDestroyed: UPDATE session_worktrees SET destroyed_at = now() WHERE session_id = ? AND worktree_path = ? AND destroyed_at IS NULL.

## Emit points (Python side)

Find where `endless worktree create` / `endless worktree drop` are implemented (probably `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py` or similar). After successful create/destroy, emit the corresponding event via the existing `event_bridge.emit_event` path.

```

Output: branch / path / created_at / destroyed_at (or "active").

You are implementing **E-1392** — landing visibility. New event kind, new materialized table, new read CLI. Implements E-1352; follows E-1322 / E-1391 pattern.

## What this is

"Landing" = a task's code actually merged to main. Distinct from:
- `task.status_changed → confirmed` (just the status flip; can happen without code landing)
- `worktree.destroyed` (filesystem cleanup; can happen on drop without landing)

Today there's no way to query "what code did session N actually ship." E-1392 fixes that.

## Three pieces

1. **New event kind**: `task.landed`. Emitted from the worktree-land path in the endless CLI when a branch successfully merges/rebases onto main.
2. **New table**: `session_landings(session_id, task_id, commit_sha, landed_at, branch_name)`. UNIQUE(session_id, task_id, commit_sha).
3. **New CLI**: `endless session landings [--session N]` listing landings attributed to a session.

## Schema (V11 migration)

```sql
CREATE TABLE session_landings (
    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    session_id  INTEGER NOT NULL,
    task_id     INTEGER NOT NULL,
    commit_sha  TEXT    NOT NULL,
    branch_name TEXT    NOT NULL,
    landed_at   TEXT    NOT NULL,
    UNIQUE(session_id, task_id, commit_sha)
);
CREATE INDEX idx_session_landings_task ON session_landings(task_id);
```

**ALL tables get a surrogate `id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY` as the first column** — Mike's 35+-year DB rule, no exceptions. The UNIQUE(session_id, task_id, commit_sha) stays as a constraint but is NOT the primary key. E-1322's session_tasks landed without an id PK; that's being fixed in E-1396. Do not repeat the omission here.

NO FK on session_id or task_id per the same rationale as session_tasks/session_worktrees (materialized index must outlive referenced entities).

## Event kind

`internal/events/event.go`:
```go
KindTaskLanded Kind = "task.landed"
```
Register in `ValidKinds`.

`internal/events/payload.go`:
```go
type TaskLandedPayload struct {
    TaskID     int64  `json:"task_id"`
    CommitSHA  string `json:"commit_sha"`
    BranchName string `json:"branch_name"`
}
```

## Handler

`internal/events/executor.go`: `execTaskLanded`. Wire to dispatch.

```go
func execTaskLanded(db dbQuerier, evt *Event) (*ExecuteResult, error) {
    var p TaskLandedPayload
    if err := json.Unmarshal(evt.Payload, &p); err != nil { ... }
    if shouldRecordSessionTouch(evt) {
        // INSERT into session_landings
    }
    return &ExecuteResult{}, nil
}
```

The session_tasks upsert from E-1322 also fires (since task.landed is a task.* event with session_id) — but that records the touch, not the landing. Both fires are correct: session_tasks tracks ALL touches; session_landings is the specific landing record.

## Emit point

`endless worktree land` is implemented in Python (probably `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py`). After the successful merge/rebase to main, emit `task.landed` with the actual commit_sha that landed (not the worktree branch's head before merge — the SHA on main after).

If the land path includes squash-merge, the commit_sha is the squash commit's SHA. If it's a fast-forward, it's the worktree branch tip.

Options:

  --since <when>
  --format [table|json]
```

Output: task ID / commit_sha (short) / branch_name / landed_at / task title (JOIN on tasks).
