Thread E-1429 DB context into _materialize_plan_file's endless-session-query call
payload.description: E-1445's `_materialize_plan_file` (worktree_cmd.py) calls `endless-session-query task-text --id N` without threading E-1429's DB context (`--db`/`--config-dir`). When `endless task claim` runs with cwd inside an endless self-dev worktree (a worktree_sandbox project), E-1429's gate refuses the DB read (exit 1: "refusing to open the database... no explicit DB context"), so the plan file is not materialized — the claim still succeeds but warns and skips. Dev-only: normal downstream users leave worktree_sandbox unset, so the gate never trips and the confirmed normal path is unaffected. Fix: thread the same `--config-dir` the Python CLI passes to other post-E-1429 Go-binary calls into the task-text invocation. Repro: from inside `.endless/worktrees/e-NNN`, run `endless-session-query task-text --id <id>` -> exit 1.

fields.outcome:
Hook regression found during verify and fixed (recovery commit bbed6cf on main): the landed E-1429 gate refused endless-hook on every UserPromptSubmit ("refusing to open the database") when the hook fired with a worktree cwd but no sandbox XDG injection -- e.g. a main-launched Claude session whose shell cd'd into the worktree. Root cause: the hook used conditional monitor.ForceRealDB(), which only redirects to main when IsSandboxActive (XDG points at a sandbox), so it no-op'd and nothing satisfied the gate. Fix: switched the hook to unconditional monitor.PinMainDB() -- always routes the DB to main AND satisfies the gate, while ConfigDir() (config.json/logs) still follows XDG. Verified by replaying the exact failing payload (claude hook, worktree cwd, no XDG): it now reaches the real DB with no refusal. Properly-spawned worktree sessions were never affected (they pin main via the sandbox XDG already).

Delivered: mandatory --db main|worktree inside worktree_sandbox self-dev worktrees, gated at the Python CLI (friendly refusal) and Go monitor.DB() (bypass-proof cwd backstop); resolved context threaded to Go subprocesses via --config-dir (never an env var); channel/tmux pin main via PinMainDB; new 'endless db path --db=main|worktree' print verb. Full verification matrix passed; all tests green (428 Python + Go).

Notes: 'db path --db=worktree' resolves to <sandbox>/endless/endless.db -- the plan's example omitted the /endless segment; the real path includes it (matches the live XDG layout). A separate half-land bug observed during land (endless worktree land advanced main before the clean-worktree check) is filed as E-1474.

payload.title: Make --db position-agnostic (any arg position) and document it in docs/guide
payload.description: E-1429's --db is a Click group option, so it only parses BEFORE the subcommand; trailing it (task show --db main) fails with "No such option" and confused a spawned agent (E-1436, 2026-05-25). The before/after split is a cognitive tax and diverges from the Go CLI port, where flags interleave freely. Make --db accepted in any position in the Python CLI (mirror the Go side's --config-dir argv pre-scan in monitor.ConsumeDBContextFlag, without swallowing the separate `db path --db`), refine the gate refusal message to show correct usage, and document --db in docs/guide (required in self-dev worktrees; main = real ledger, worktree = sandbox). The intentional friction stays: the caller must still choose main|worktree -- this only removes the placement gotcha.

fields.description: Unify the E-1429 --db flag: kill `db path`'s separate --db and have db path use the one global --db (both answer the same question -- which DB). Rename the choice value worktree->sandbox (clearer for the user's intent; the landed code still uses worktree everywhere -- global flag, db path flag, refusal message, apply_db_choice). Make the single --db position-agnostic -- accept it before OR after the subcommand -- so there is no before/after cognitive split and it ports cleanly to the planned Go CLI (the real reason: Python->Go portability before distribution). Refine the gate refusal message and document --db in docs/guide/ (required inside self-dev worktrees; main = real ledger, sandbox = this worktree's throwaway test DB). Intentional friction stays: the caller must still choose main|sandbox; only the placement gotcha and the duplicate flag go away. Unifying to one --db also removes the earlier pre-scan wrinkle -- nothing left to mis-grab.

fields.title: Unify --db into one position-agnostic flag (rename worktree value to sandbox); document in guide
Add internal/monitor and the task-text query logic to the just test-go sweep
fields.description: `just test-go` runs only internal/kairos, internal/events, and cmd/endless-sandbox. It omits internal/monitor (e.g. monitor.TaskText, the E-1445 plan-text reader) and the endless-session-query / task-text read logic, so that code has no automated Go test. Add the missing packages to the Go test sweep. The original build-recipe half of this task (`just go` building only a subset of binaries) is DROPPED: E-1367's consolidation into a single endless-go binary subsumes it (one build target can't omit a subset). Blocked by E-1367 because consolidation relocates the query logic into the unified binary, changing which package the sweep must target. Note: E-1367's current scope list omits endless-session-query — fold it in there when planning E-1367.

fields.text:
# E-1476 — Unify `--db` into one position-agnostic flag (rename `worktree`→`sandbox`); document in guide

## Context
E-1429 (landed) added a `--db main|worktree` gate: endless CLI commands run inside a self-dev worktree must explicitly choose the real ledger vs the per-worktree sandbox DB. The friction is **intentional** — forcing the choice prevents silently writing to the wrong DB. Two rough edges remain (hit by a spawned session on E-1436, 2026-05-25):

1. **Two `--db` flags** — the root group's `--db` AND `db path`'s own `--db`. They answer the same question ("which DB"), so there should be one.
2. **Position-sensitive** — `--db` is a Click *group* option, so it only parses *before* the subcommand (`endless --db main task show`); the natural `endless task show --db main` fails with "No such option '--db'". That's a cognitive tax (users must know which flags are global) and won't survive the planned Python→Go port (Go CLIs accept flags in any position).

**Outcome:** one `--db`, accepted in any argument position, value renamed `worktree`→`sandbox` (clearer: the throwaway test DB), choice still mandatory (no default, never an env var), documented in the guide.

## Approach
Mirror the Go side's "consume the flag from anywhere at entry" pattern (`monitor.ConsumeDBContextFlag`, internal/monitor/db.go:153) by overriding the Click group's `main()` to pre-extract `--db` from argv before normal parsing. `CliRunner.invoke` calls `cli.main(args=...)` (click/testing.py:501), so this single override makes `--db` position-agnostic for **both** the real process and the test suite — **no pyproject entry-point change, single extraction site**.

### 1. `src/endless/cli.py`
- Add `import sys` and a `DBAwareGroup(click.Group)` overriding `main(self, args=None, **extra)`:
  - `argv = list(args) if args is not None else sys.argv[1:]`
  - Scan for `--db <val>` and `--db=<val>` **anywhere**; build a cleaned list without them; capture the value (last-wins, matching Go's loop).
  - If captured: `try: config.apply_db_choice(val) except ValueError as e: raise click.UsageError(str(e))`. If `--db` is the last token with no value → `raise click.UsageError("--db requires a value: main or sandbox")`.
  - `return super().main(args=cleaned, **extra)`
- `@click.group()` → `@click.group(cls=DBAwareGroup)`. **Remove** the `--db` option + `db_choice` param from `main()` and its in-handler `apply_db_choice` block (the override owns it now — single source). Add an `epilog=` documenting `--db main|sandbox` so `endless --help` still surfaces it (the override consumes the flag, so a normal Click option would be vestigial).
- `db path` (cli.py:1912-1941): **remove** its `--db` option/param. New handler:
  - `from endless import config`
  - `if config.RESOLVED_CONFIG_DIR is None: raise click.ClickException("db path needs an explicit --db value: 'endless db path --db=main' or 'endless db path --db=sandbox'.")`
  - `click.echo(str(config.DB_PATH))`
  - Update docstring (drop `--db=worktree`; note it prints the path for the globally-selected `--db`).

### 2. `src/endless/config.py`
- `apply_db_choice` (232): `elif choice == "worktree"` → `"sandbox"`; reword its ValueError to `"--db sandbox only applies inside a self-dev worktree (.endless/worktrees/e-NNN); cwd is not in one"`; the `else: raise ValueError("unknown --db value: ...")` branch is now the real validator (drop `# pragma: no cover`); docstring → `--db main|sandbox`.
- `WORKTREE_DB_REFUSAL` (168-173) — rewrite body (constant name stays):
  ```
  running inside a self-dev worktree requires an explicit --db value (accepted in any position):

    --db main     the real ledger — managing the project
    --db sandbox  this worktree's throwaway test DB — testing endless itself

  Need paths? Run `endless db path --db=main|sandbox`.
  ```
- Fix `--db main|worktree` in the doc comments (~line 21, ~155-160) → `--db main|sandbox`.

### 3. `internal/monitor/db.go`
- `worktreeDBContextRefusal` (~223): user-facing `--db main|worktree` → `--db main|sandbox` (Go has no flag *value* to rename; this string is the only edit). `just build` to refresh the binaries.

### 4. `docs/guide/orchestration.md`
- Add `### Choosing the database (--db)` under `## Worktrees`, after `### Getting into the worktree` (~line 43): required only inside a self-dev worktree of a `worktree_sandbox` project; `main` = real ledger (`~/.config/endless/endless.db`), `sandbox` = this worktree's throwaway DB (`<cache>/endless/sandboxes/worktree-e-<id>/`); mandatory by design (no default, never an env var — an exported var would silently route every later command to the wrong DB); position-agnostic; `endless db path --db=main|sandbox` prints a path without opening the DB.

### 5. `tests/test_db_gate.py`
- Value rename: `apply_db_choice("worktree")`→`"sandbox"` (lines 58, 64); `--db=worktree`→`--db=sandbox` (127, 135); refusal-text asserts (81-82) → `--db main` / `--db sandbox`; tighten `test_db_path_requires_db_flag` to assert "needs an explicit --db".
- New tests (CliRunner now covers both positions via the override):
  - position-agnostic: `invoke(main, ["--db=main","db","path"])` and `invoke(main, ["db","path","--db=main"])` both print the main path (reset `config.RESOLVED_CONFIG_DIR=None` between).
  - `db path` with no `--db` → ClickException "needs an explicit --db".
  - `--db sandbox` outside a worktree → exit≠0, "self-dev worktree".
  - `--db worktree` (old value) → exit≠0, "unknown --db value" (proves no alias).

### Not changed
- `pyproject.toml` entry point (stays `endless.cli:main`).
- `db.py:get_db`, `event_bridge.py`, `session_cmd.py`, `cli.py:serve` — they use `require_db_context`/`go_db_context_args`, unchanged.
- Concept names kept: `sandbox_config_dir`, `worktree_task_id`, `gated_worktree_root`, `WORKTREE_DB_REFUSAL` (name), `worktree_sandbox` config key, `worktree-e-NNN` dir naming.

## Reused functions
`config.apply_db_choice` (single resolver, now `main|sandbox`), `config.set_db_context`, `config.main_config_dir`, `config.sandbox_config_dir`, `config.worktree_task_id`, `config.gated_worktree_root`, `config.require_db_context`, `config.go_db_context_args` — all unchanged except the value rename in `apply_db_choice`. Mirrors `monitor.ConsumeDBContextFlag` (Go).

## Critical files
- `src/endless/cli.py` (DBAwareGroup, group, `db path`)
- `src/endless/config.py` (`apply_db_choice`, `WORKTREE_DB_REFUSAL`, comments)
- `internal/monitor/db.go` (`worktreeDBContextRefusal` string)
- `docs/guide/orchestration.md` (new `--db` subsection)
- `tests/test_db_gate.py`

## Verification
Implement in a worktree (`endless task claim E-1476`); worktree-gated checks run from inside `.endless/worktrees/e-*` of this repo (a `worktree_sandbox` project). `just build` (picks up the Go string + binaries); Python is live via editable install.

From a non-worktree dir:
- `endless --db main db path` / `endless db path --db main` / `endless db path --db=main` → all print `~/.config/endless/endless.db` (position-agnostic).
- `endless db path` → fails "needs an explicit --db".
- `cd /tmp && endless db path --db sandbox` → fails "self-dev worktree".

From inside a self-dev worktree:
- `endless db path --db sandbox` → `<cache>/endless/sandboxes/worktree-e-<id>/endless/endless.db`.
- `endless db path --db worktree` → fails (unknown value; no alias).
- `endless task list` → refusal (sandbox wording, "any position").
- `endless --db main task list` AND `endless task list --db main` → both succeed (position-agnostic through the gate).
- `endless --help` → epilog shows `--db main|sandbox`.

Tests: `just test` (Python) + `go test ./internal/monitor/...` (gate tests; not in `just test-go`) + `just test-go`.

# E-1446 plan: Include worktree slug in sandbox dir name for 1-to-1 worktree↔sandbox mapping

## Context

Today the sandbox dir name is built from `task_id` only — `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py:906` is `name = f"worktree-e-{task_id}"`. After E-1447 lands, this becomes `name = f"e-{task_id}"` but still uses task_id only. The worktree dir's optional slug suffix (`e-{task_id}-{slug}`) is collapsed away.

Consequence: multiple worktrees for the same task (e.g., one for production work, one for testing) all map to a single sandbox and conflate their DB state. Each worktree should get its own sandbox dir matching its worktree dir name 1-to-1.

Assumes E-1429 has landed (`endless --db main|sandbox ...` routing flag is in place).

## Prerequisite

**E-1447 must have landed** (linked `E-1446 blocked_by E-1447`). This plan starts from the post-E-1447 shape: `name = f"e-{task_id}"`.

## Implementation site

`src/endless/worktree_cmd.py:906` — post-E-1447 reads:

```python
name = f"e-{task_id}"
```

Change to:

```python
name = worktree_path.name
```

`worktree_path` is the `Path` already passed into `_maybe_auto_sandbox_bind` (signature: `def _maybe_auto_sandbox_bind(project_root: Path, worktree_path: Path, task_id: int)`). `.name` returns the final directory component — `e-{task_id}` if no slug, `e-{task_id}-{slug}` if the worktree was created with one. The `task_id` parameter becomes unused at this call site; remove it from the signature if it has no other use, otherwise leave it.

`cmd/endless-sandbox/bind.go:99` already does `filepath.Base(worktree)` after E-1447 — same semantic. No change needed there.

## Migration of existing sandboxes

Same approach as E-1447: existing sandboxes are NOT renamed. Live Claude sessions captured `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` at process spawn, so renaming mid-session would either fail-on-open or cause SQLite to silently re-create an empty DB at the old path. New worktrees get sandboxes named after their worktree dir verbatim. Existing `e-{task_id}/` dirs (from the post-E-1447, pre-E-1446 window) stay until their worktree is dropped, then the user runs `endless-sandbox destroy e-{task_id}` as today.

## Verification

1. Build (`just build`).
2. Multi-worktree case — the core scenario this task fixes:
   ```sh
   # Worktree A for task E-NNN, no slug:
   endless --db main task claim E-NNN
   # Worktree B for same task with a slug (e.g., 'testing'):
   git worktree add .endless/worktrees/e-NNN-testing main
   cd .endless/worktrees/e-NNN-testing
   just dev-sandbox-init
   ls ~/.cache/endless/sandboxes/
   # Expected: e-NNN/ AND e-NNN-testing/ (two distinct sandboxes, not one shared)
   ```
3. Each sandbox has its own DB; writes to one don't appear in the other:
   ```sh
   # From worktree A — write a probe row into A's sandbox DB:
   endless --db sandbox task add "Check probe isolation" --type task --no-create-worktree
   # From worktree B — read from B's sandbox DB; A's probe must not appear:
   endless --db sandbox sql "SELECT count(*) FROM tasks WHERE title = 'Check probe isolation'"
   # Expected: 0 (the probe is in A's sandbox, not B's)
   ```
4. `just test` — all tests pass.

## Closing

After verification, file outcome and flip to `verify`. Do not auto-land — Mike confirms.

## Related

- **E-1447** — prerequisite (`E-1446 blocked_by E-1447`). Drops the `worktree-` prefix; this task changes the source from `task_id` to `worktree_path.name` on top of that.
- E-1429 — explicit `--db main|sandbox` routing. Assumed landed; this plan uses `--db main` in verification.
- E-1456 — config key rename. Independent.
- E-1281 — original sandbox-routing feature.

payload.title: Remove 'Blocks:'/'Blocked by:' tasks from 'Relates to:' in 'task show' output
payload.description: When a task pair has a blocks/blocked_by relation AND a separate relates_to row, the pair shows under both the 'Blocks:'/'Blocked by:' header and the 'Relates to:' header in task show / task relations output. The block already conveys the pair is related, so listing it again under 'Relates to:' reads as a redundant duplicate link and is misleading. Suppress a task from the 'Relates to:' section when the same pair already appears under 'Blocks:' or 'Blocked by:'.

fields.title: Make worktree land tolerate a post-ff-merge task.landed emit failure (main already advanced)
fields.description: Investigation (2026-05-26) corrected the original diagnosis: worktree land's clean-worktree guard (_guard_dirty_worktree, Step 3.8) already runs BEFORE the ff-merge (Step 5), so it does not advance main before checking. The real gap: the ff-merge (Step 5) advances main, then Step 6 emits task.landed; if that emit fails (it did, on E-1470's session-attribution gate), main is advanced but task_landings gets no row AND the error reads as if the land never happened. Fix: in land_worktree, wrap the post-ff-merge task.landed emit so a failure surfaces "Landed <id> (main advanced) but recording the landing failed: <cause>; re-run just land <id> to record it (the ff-merge is idempotent)" instead of re-raising as total failure. Narrow hardening; the trigger we hit (attribution) is removed by E-1470, but other emit failures would leave the same partial state.

payload.title: Render task relations as a single 'Links:' line with per-item relation type in parens
payload.description: Replace the per-type relation headers (Blocks:/Blocked by:/Relates to:/Enables:/Enabled by:/etc.) with one 'Links:' label across task show, task detail, AND the dedicated task relations/deps command. Each entry lists the related task id with its relation type in parens, directional from the subject task, e.g.  Links: E-1429 (relates to), E-1447 (blocks). Each dep_type maps to its directional human label (blocks/blocked by, relates to, cleans up/cleaned up by, replaces/replaced by, enables/enabled by). The grouped-by-type view is removed entirely; the single Links line is the only relation format.

fields.text:
# E-1436: Implement `endless task next revise` command

## Spec source

E-1421's plan at `<main>/.endless/plans/E-1421.md`. Relevant sections: "CLI surface" → revise, "Concurrency model", "JSON shape (input/output)", "Confirmed design decisions" (soft cap >10 warning, hard cap >25 refusal).

## Scope

Implement `endless task next revise --file <json>` — full-rewrite of the curated list:

1. Read JSON from `--file`.
2. Validate schema (lanes, integer priorities, items, soft cap >10 warning, hard cap >25 refusal).
3. Read latest `revised_at` + `session_id` from `project_next_revisions`. Print "list last revised Xh ago by session N" (or "first revision") for cross-session collision visibility.
4. `BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION`.
5. Delete existing `project_next_lanes` and `project_next_items` for this project (CASCADE handles items via the lanes FK).
6. Insert new lanes and items from the JSON.
7. Insert a `change_kind='revise'` row in `project_next_revisions` with `session_id` (from the resolver), `revised_at=NOW`, `json_snapshot` = input JSON.
8. `COMMIT`.
9. Emit `--json` of the resulting state.

On `SQLITE_BUSY` (concurrent revise/move): surface the conflict clearly and exit non-zero.

## Out of scope

- Read command (E-1440), move (E-1438), hooks (E-1437), briefing (E-1439).

## Verification

1. Revise from scratch (empty list): JSON with 2 lanes, 3 items each → rows appear; revision row in audit table.
2. Revise existing list (replace): different JSON → old lanes/items gone, new ones in; second revision row appended.
3. Soft cap: 11 items → warning printed, command succeeds.
4. Hard cap: 26 items → command rejects with error before BEGIN.
5. Collision message: revise twice from different sessions; second prints "list last revised <seconds> ago by session N" before applying.
6. Concurrent BEGIN IMMEDIATE conflict (simulated): one wins, other gets clear conflict error.
7. `--json` output matches DB state after.

## Instructions

Read E-1421's plan for design. No separate plan file needed. Flip to `verify` with the verification block in the handoff.

## Implementation decisions (resolved with Mike, 2026-05-26)

The spec predates the event-sourced write path, so three points were decided in chat:

1. **Caps + structure validation live in the Go executor (write-path gate), not the CLI.** `endless-event` validates structure and the >25 hard cap *before* taking the lock (refusal writes nothing); the >10 soft cap returns a warning the CLI prints. Per gates-not-guardrails.
2. **The collision read is done in Go, printed by the CLI just after.** The executor reads the prior revision inside the transaction and returns `{revised_at, session_id}`; the CLI prints "list last revised Xh ago by session N" (or "first revision"). Keeps DB reads in Go (E-894); a single CLI call has no abort window before BEGIN, so "just after" is equivalent.
3. **Lock-first ordering (mirrors the create path).** A new `events.BeginImmediate()` takes `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` *before* the events-authoritative JSONL ledger append, so a losing concurrent revise fails at BEGIN (SQLITE_BUSY) leaving no orphan ledger line and no audit row.

## Implementation summary

- `internal/events/event.go` — `KindProjectNextRevised` + `EntityProjectNext` (registered in `ValidKinds`/`validEntityTypes`).
- `internal/events/payload.go` — `ProjectNextRevisedPayload` (lanes/items; `pending_triage` ignored — hook-managed).
- `internal/events/project_next.go` — `ValidateProjectNextRevise` (caps gate), `execProjectNextRevised` (get-or-create header, capture prior revision, full lane/item rewrite, `change_kind='revise'` audit row), result structs.
- `internal/events/executor.go` — `BeginImmediate()` lock-first helper, `ExecuteResult.ProjectNext`, dispatch case.
- `cmd/endless-event/main.go` — `runProjectNextRevise` (validate-before-lock → BEGIN IMMEDIATE → ledger append → execute → COMMIT → emit `{ts,kind,prior_revision,warning,state}`).
- `src/endless/cli.py` — `task next` is now a group (`invoke_without_command=True`, bare command unchanged) hosting `revise --file [--project] [--json]`.
- `src/endless/task_cmd.py` — `revise_next_list` + `_format_relative`. `--json` keeps stdout pure JSON (advisory lines to stderr).
- Tests: `internal/events/project_next_test.go` (executor, validation/caps, BEGIN IMMEDIATE conflict), `tests/test_task_next_revise.py` (CLI wiring). No schema change (E-1434 added the tables).

payload.title: Surface task landings in task show output and add a task landed read command
payload.description: Make the git-landing data that 'endless worktree land' already records visible in the task read CLI. Each land writes a task_landings row (landed_at, branch, merge SHA); none is surfaced today. Add a 'Landed:' line to task show (most recent landed_at + latest short merge SHA, with a '(landed N times)' suffix when >1 landing) across default/--llm/--json outputs. Add 'task landed': bare lists tasks that have landed (newest first); 'task landed <id>' shows that task's full landing history. Per decision with Mike, all landing reads ride on E-894's Go read path (query_bridge.py pilot, E-1053), not Python db.query — hence blocked on E-894.
payload.text:
# Surface task landings in the read CLI

## Goal

Make the git-landing data that `endless worktree land` already records visible
in the task read surface. Every land emits a `task.landed` event; the Go
projector writes one row per land into the `task_landings` table
(`task_id`, `session_id`, `branch`, `merge_commit_sha`, `landed_at`). Landing
is append-only (a task can land more than once) and does NOT change task status.
None of this is surfaced in the CLI today.

## Decisions (made with Mike, 2026-05-26)

1. **`Landed:` line in `task show`** — most recent `landed_at` (formatted by
   `_format_timestamp`, like the existing `Confirmed:` line) + the latest
   land's short merge SHA, with a `(landed N times)` suffix only when more than
   one landing row exists. Shown only when at least one landing row exists.
   Example: `Landed:       2026-05-25 4:09 pm  a1b2c3d  (landed 2 times)`
   Add to all three outputs: default human, `--llm`, and `--json`.

2. **`endless task landed` command (arg-driven)**:
   - Bare `task landed` → lists tasks that have at least one landing, ordered by
     most-recent `landed_at` descending (peer to `task active` / `task recent`).
   - `task landed <id>` → that task's full landing history: every row
     (`landed_at`, `branch`, `merge_commit_sha`), newest first.

3. **DB read path** — all landing reads ride on E-894's Go read path
   (the `query_bridge.py` pilot, E-1053), NOT a new Python `db.query`. This is
   why the work is blocked on E-894 advancing rather than shipped now against
   the current Python read code.

## Insertion points (current code, for reference)

- `src/endless/task_cmd.py` `detail_item()`:
  - default human render: after the `Confirmed:` line (~L2893)
  - `--llm`: after `confirmed=` (~L2845)
  - `--json`: after `"confirmed"` key (~L2806)
- New `task landed` command registered in `src/endless/cli.py` task group
  (pattern: see `task release` / `task confirm`), calling a new
  `landed_item(item_id)` / `landed_list()` in `task_cmd.py`.
- Data: `task_landings` (schema.sql). Latest-per-task via
  `MAX(landed_at)` / `landed_at DESC` (indexed `idx_task_landings_task`).

## Blocked by

E-894 (move task-display reads to Go) → which relates to E-1053 (define the
`query_bridge.py` pilot pattern, currently needs_plan). Implement the landing
reads on that Go path once it exists.

## Verification (when unblocked)

- Land a task, then `endless task show <id>` shows the `Landed:` line with the
  right timestamp + short SHA; re-land and confirm `(landed 2 times)`.
- `endless task landed` lists it; `endless task landed <id>` shows both rows.
- `--llm` and `--json` carry the landing fields.

fields.text:
# E-1477 — Render task relations as a single "Links:" section

## Context

Task relations today render as a stack of per-type headers — `Blocks:`, `Blocked by:`,
`Relates to:`, `Cleans up:`, `Replaced by:`, etc. — one heading per relation type, in
three CLI surfaces. That's verbose and forces the reader to scan multiple headings to
learn "what is this task linked to." E-1477 replaces all of it with a single **`Links:`**
section where each entry is the related task id followed by its directional relation type
in parens. The grouped-by-type view is removed entirely; the single Links section is the
only relation format.

The data layer is untouched — `get_all_relations()`, `CANONICAL_DEP_TYPES`,
`RELATION_LABELS`, and `STORED_DEP_TYPES` all stay as-is. This is purely a rendering
change. Directionality is already resolved by `get_all_relations()` (it picks the
display-name based on whether the subject task is the source or target of each row), and
`RELATION_LABELS[name]` already gives the directional human label — we just lowercase it
for the parenthetical.

## Decisions (resolved with user)

- **Three CLI surfaces, two functions, one file** (`src/endless/task_cmd.py`):
  - `detail_item()` serves both `task show` and `task detail` (`detail` is an alias of
    `show`, cli.py:882). Also serves `decision show` (cli.py:1466) — decisions get the
    same Links rendering, no special-casing.
  - `show_relations()` serves both `task deps` and `task relations` (cli.py:1366, 1376).
  - The web UI (`internal/web/*.templ`) only renders `BlockedBy` and is **out of scope** —
    not named in the task. No Go changes.
- **Human view (both `task show`/`detail` AND `task relations`/`deps`): multi-line, rich
  rows, rendered identically.** A cyan `Links:` heading, then one indented line per
  relation — `E-NNNN (relation type) [status] title` — id-ascending, with the status
  colored (green if terminal, else yellow):

  ```
  Links:
    E-1429 (relates to) [confirmed] Some title
    E-1447 (blocks) [ready] Another title
  ```

  Factored into **one shared helper** so the two surfaces can't drift.
- **`--llm` outputs switch to a single compact Links line** (machine output favors
  compactness; user-approved):
  - `task show --llm`: `links=E-1429 (relates to),E-1447 (blocks)` (key=value, comma, no
    space — matches the surrounding `key=value` block).
  - `task relations --llm`: keep the `# Relations for E-NNN` header, then
    `Links: E-1429 (relates to), E-1447 (blocks)`.
- **Parenthetical label = `RELATION_LABELS[name].lower()`** → "relates to", "blocked by",
  "cleans up", "replaced by", matching the spec's example. Leaves `RELATION_LABELS`
  capitalized so `test_cleans_up_in_canonical_registries` stays green.
- **Ordering: id-ascending** across all relations (matches the spec example, where
  `E-1429 (relates to)` precedes `E-1447 (blocks)` despite `relates_to` sorting last in
  `RELATION_DISPLAY_ORDER`).
- **Empty relations:** `detail_item` prints nothing (as today); `show_relations` prints
  `(none)` (as today).

## Files to modify

- `src/endless/task_cmd.py` — the only source file.
- `tests/test_relations.py` — add rendering tests.

## Implementation

### 1. Module-level helpers (near `get_all_relations`)

```python
# Statuses that count as "done" for relation-row coloring (E-1477).
_RELATION_TERMINAL_STATUSES = ("confirmed", "assumed", "completed", "declined", "obsolete")


def _flatten_relations(item_id: int) -> list[dict]:
    """Flatten get_all_relations into one id-ascending list, each entry carrying the
    directional, lower-cased relation label, for the unified 'Links:' rendering (E-1477)."""
    flat: list[dict] = []
    for display_name, items in get_all_relations(item_id).items():
        rel = RELATION_LABELS.get(display_name, display_name).lower()
        for d in items:
            flat.append({"id": d["id"], "rel": rel,
                         "status": d["status"], "title": d["title"]})
    flat.sort(key=lambda r: r["id"])
    return flat


def _echo_links_section(item_id: int) -> bool:
    """Emit the unified multi-line 'Links:' section (E-1477): a cyan 'Links:' heading,
    then one indented, colored row per relation (id-ascending) —
    'E-NNN (relation type) [status] title'. Emits nothing and returns False when the
    task has no relations; returns True otherwise. Shared by task show/detail and
    relations/deps so both render identically."""
    links = _flatten_relations(item_id)
    if not links:
        return False
    click.echo(click.style("Links:", fg="cyan"))
    for r in links:
        color = "green" if r["status"] in _RELATION_TERMINAL_STATUSES else "yellow"
        click.echo(
            f"  {task_id_display(r['id'])} ({r['rel']}) "
            f"[{click.style(r['status'], fg=color)}] {r['title']}")
    return True
```

### 2. `detail_item()` — human view (currently lines 2799-2803)

Replace the per-display-name loop with the shared helper. It slots in where the old
relation lines were (between `Parent:` and `Created:`):

```python
_echo_links_section(item_id)
```

Also reduce `col_w` from 13 → 11 and fix its comment (line 2779): the per-type labels
that justified width 13 are gone; the longest remaining padded label is now `Confirmed:`
(10 chars + 1 space). (The `Links:` heading is no longer in the padded label column.)

### 3. `detail_item()` — `--llm` view (currently lines 2753-2756)

```python
links = _flatten_relations(item_id)
if links:
    link_str = ",".join(f"E-{r['id']} ({r['rel']})" for r in links)
    click.echo(f"links={link_str}")
```

### 4. `show_relations()` — human view (currently lines 3693-3715)

Replace the local `terminal` tuple, the per-display-name grouped loop, and the empty
branch with the shared helper:

```python
click.echo()
click.echo(click.style(f"Relations for {task_id_display(item_id)}", fg="green", bold=True))
click.echo(click.style("─" * 30, dim=True))
if not _echo_links_section(item_id):
    click.echo("  (none)")
click.echo()
```

### 5. `show_relations()` — `--llm` view (currently lines 3681-3691)

```python
if llm:
    click.echo(f"# Relations for E-{item_id}")
    links = _flatten_relations(item_id)
    if not links:
        click.echo("(none)")
        return
    link_str = ", ".join(f"E-{r['id']} ({r['rel']})" for r in links)
    click.echo(f"Links: {link_str}")
    return
```

## Tests (`tests/test_relations.py`)

Existing tests are unaffected (`get_all_relations` and `RELATION_LABELS` unchanged). Add:

- **`_flatten_relations`**: seed `a blocks b`, `c blocks a` (→ `a blocked_by c`),
  `a relates_to b`; assert the returned list is id-ascending and labels are lowercased
  directional (`"blocked by"`, `"blocks"`, `"relates to"`).
- **`task show` human** (CliRunner `task show <a>`): output has a `Links:` heading, rows
  `E-b (blocks) [...]` and `E-c (blocked by) [...]` carrying status + title, and no old
  per-type headings (`"Blocks:"`, `"Blocked by:"`).
- **`task show --llm`**: output has a `links=` line, no `blocked_by=` lines.
- **`task relations` human** (CliRunner `task relations <a>`): one `Links:` heading,
  rich rows; no per-type headings.
- **`task relations --llm`**: contains a `Links: ` line with the parenthetical labels.

Use the existing `_add_task` / `_seed_project` / CliRunner-on-`endless.cli:main`
patterns already in the file.

## Verification

1. `just test` (or focused: `uv run pytest tests/test_relations.py -q`) — all green.
2. Live read against a task that has relations, using `--db main` (read-only): find an id
   with multiple links via `endless task relations <id> --db main`, then eyeball:
   - `endless task show <id> --db main` → `Links:` section, multi-line rich rows.
   - `endless task relations <id> --db main` → identical `Links:` section.
   - both with `--llm` → the compact machine Links formats above.
   If no task with varied relations exists, seed one in `--db sandbox`
   (`endless task add ... --db sandbox`, then `endless task link ... --db sandbox`).

payload.title: Add --record-only flag to endless worktree land for registering already-completed lands
payload.description: When a worktree was landed manually (or before task_landings existed), there's no clean path today to register the task.landed event after the fact. Add --record-only to 'endless worktree land' that emits the task.landed event with the supplied --branch / --sha / --at and skips all the rebase / dedup / orphan-drop / fast-forward work. First use case: backfill the missing task_landings row for E-1402, which landed on 2026-05-17 before the table existed.

fields.description: When a worktree was landed manually (or before task_landings existed), there's no clean path today to register the task.landed event after the fact. Add --record-only to 'endless worktree land' that emits the task.landed event with the user-supplied --branch / --sha / --at and skips all the rebase / dedup / orphan-drop / fast-forward work. --at is required (no default-to-now) so the flag can't be misused as a shortcut for live lands. First use case: backfill the missing task_landings row for E-1402, which landed on 2026-05-17 before the table existed.

fields.text:
You are picking up E-1479. Goal: add a `--record-only` flag to `endless worktree land` that emits a `task.landed` event for a land that already happened, without doing any of the rebase / dedup / orphan-drop / fast-forward work.

## Why this exists

`task_landings` is the projection of `task.landed` events. The projector at `internal/events/projector.go:388` inserts one row per event. Today the only way to get that event emitted is to run `endless worktree land` end-to-end — which requires the worktree to still exist and the branch to still be in a landable state.

When a land happened outside that flow — manual `git merge`, a land that completed before `task_landings` existed (E-1402, 2026-05-17), tooling experiments — there's no path to register it after the fact. Going direct-INSERT into `task_landings` bypasses the event-sourced contract and leaves the ledger silent about a real land. We want a single command surface that respects the event ledger but skips the work.

## Design constraints (already decided)

- **Flag, not subcommand.** `endless worktree land --record-only` — not `endless worktree record-landing`. Two-segment verbs are a CLI smell in this project, and `--help` discoverability on the existing `land` verb wins over adding a sibling verb. The "only" in the flag name carries the "skip the work" semantic clearly.

- **`--at` is required.** No default-to-now. Forcing the user to type a historical timestamp adds friction in the right place: it disambiguates "this land happened at a specific past time" from "I want to skip the rebase that should happen now." Live lands always have `landed_at = now()` via the normal path; the only legitimate use for `--record-only` is registering something that already happened.

- **`--branch` and `--sha` are also required.** Neither can be inferred when the work hasn't been done. The worktree may not even exist anymore.

## Phase 1 — investigation

- Read the current `land_worktree` codepath in `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py` (start near line 1160 — the `task.landed` emit) and trace what envelope fields the event carries today.
- Read `internal/events/projector.go:380-396` to confirm exactly what the projector consumes from the event payload + envelope. The required fields for a successful insert are: task_id (entity_id), branch + merge_commit_sha (payload), landed_at (envelope ts). `session_id` comes from `actor.session_id` and is allowed to be NULL.
- Read `internal/events/payload.go:62-71` for the `TaskLandedPayload` contract and the note about pre-bridge calls leaving `session_id` NULL.
- Find the click command at the CLI layer that invokes `land_worktree`. Note its current option surface.
- Confirm: does anything else in the land flow have side effects that should run even in `--record-only` mode (auto-cleanup of orphan branches, session-binding release, anything Endless-managed)? If yes, list them and surface in Phase 2.

## Phase 2 — design

Produce a plan file at `.endless/plans/E-1479.md` covering, at minimum:

1. **Option surface.** Final flag names, requireds, validation rules. Spell out the exact error messages for: missing required arg combined with `--record-only`, `--record-only` plus an arg the normal flow doesn't accept, a positional `TASK_ID` shape that conflicts with anything (none expected).

2. **Validation interactions.** What happens with `--record-only` combined with any other land flag (today's land verb accepts which?). Reject combinations that don't make sense; document the exact rule.

3. **`--at` parsing.** Accept what format? Recommended: ISO-8601 with second precision (`2026-05-17T22:48:59`), matching the schema's default. Should `--at` accept timezone offsets, or assume the project's TZ (UTC?)? Look at how `landed_at` is stored today before deciding.

4. **Session attribution.** The event envelope's `actor.session_id` populates `task_landings.session_id` via the projector. For `--record-only`, should it use the current resolved session (whoever is running the command, via the usual resolver), accept `--session-id` explicitly, or always leave NULL? Each has a story:
   - Resolver-derived: matches normal flow; means the recording session gets credit.
   - Explicit: lets the user say "the land happened from session 461" if they know.
   - Always NULL: the record-only act isn't a real land, so attribution is fiction.
   Present pros/cons; **do not choose — ask Mike.**

5. **Idempotency.** What if a `task.landed` row already exists for this task with this sha? Refuse, append (the projector appends today — re-landings produce multiple rows per E-1337), or no-op? `internal/events/payload.go:62-71` says re-landing appends; the question is whether `--record-only` should follow that or refuse on duplicate.

6. **What the user sees on success.** One-line summary or the same multi-line output as a normal land? Default to a minimal "Recorded landing of E-NNN at <ts> (branch X, sha Y)" since no actual work was done.

7. **Tests.** Coverage proposal:
   - happy path: --record-only + required args + new task → event written + row in `task_landings` with expected fields.
   - missing required arg → clear error, no event written.
   - invalid `--at` format → clear error, no event written.
   - invoking without `--record-only` continues to behave exactly as today (regression guard).
   - whichever idempotency rule was chosen (Phase 2.5) gets a test.

Each open question gets pros/cons. **DO NOT CHOOSE the open ones — present to Mike.**

## Phase 3 — implementation and closure

Once Mike approves the plan:

- Implement on `task/1479-...`.
- Add Phase 2.7's tests. Verify with `just test`.
- After it lands, run the backfill it was filed for:
  ```
  endless worktree land E-1402 --record-only \
    --branch task/1402-strengthen-session-resolver-handle-multi \
    --sha 33a2ea48ef1a2cfefdd9d5d86e1eef5a410434b5 \
    --at 2026-05-17T22:48:59
  ```
  Confirm `endless sql "SELECT * FROM task_landings WHERE task_id = 1402"` returns one row with the expected values. That's the close-out for E-1402's land hygiene.

## Non-goals

- Backfilling other historically-missing `task_landings` rows beyond E-1402. If Mike wants a bulk backfill, file a separate task.
- Touching the normal land flow's behavior. `--record-only` is strictly additive.
- Renaming or restructuring `task_landings`.
- Adding a `--record-only` knob to commands other than `worktree land`.

## Closing

- Set status to `verify` when implementation is done. Include in the verify handoff: the exact backfill command above, and the `endless sql` to confirm the row landed.
- Do NOT auto-land. Ask Mike.

payload.title: Update guide index relation-display line to describe the Links: section
Add goal_task_id to sessions: preserve session purpose separate from active focus
payload.description: Sessions have only active_task_id, which both 'claim' and 'bind' overwrite, so a session loses the task it was opened for once focus shifts (this very session: opened on E-1402, planning E-1479, with a misleading tmux bar). Add a nullable goal_task_id, immutable after first set, written alongside active_task_id by 'claim' and by a new 'bind --include-goal' (for the case claim was never run); render the tmux status as [E-NNNN] when goal==active or goal is null, and [E-GOAL/E-ACTIVE] when they diverge. Full design (invariant enforcement location, migration, exact status-bar divergence format, claim-when-goal-already-set behavior) to be resolved in this task's planning session per house rule that plans carry no open questions.

fields.text:
# E-894 — Move task-display DB reads from Python to Go

> Refreshed 2026-05-26 against current `main` (HEAD survey). Supersedes the
> 2026-05-02 plan, whose "focus pilot" template and `migrate.go` framework
> assumptions are stale (see Reconciliation below).

## Goal

Move every task-display DB **read** out of Python and into Go, so Python has
zero SQLite knowledge for task operations. Python keeps rendering (Rich tables,
markdown, tree, `--json`, `--llm`); only the data-fetch step moves. Decoupling
scope: **full** (Phases 1-3) per decision with Mike 2026-05-26 — also retire
Python's schema bootstrap so Go owns DB creation.

## Reconciliation with current main (what changed since 2026-05-02)

- **No "focus pilot" to mirror.** The old plan said it would copy an established
  `query_bridge.py` + `internal/events/query.go` + `endless-event get-focus`
  read pattern. That code was never landed (stranded uncommitted in an orphaned
  `e-1031` worktree) and is abandoned. We build the read pattern **fresh**,
  mirroring the **write** bridge (`event_bridge.py`) instead. E-1053, which
  formalized that pilot, is obsoleted.
- **Schema framework is gone.** E-1459 deleted the E-863 `migrate.go` versioned
  framework and replaced it with `internal/schema/schema.sql` (authoritative,
  applied by Go on every `monitor.DB()` open) + one-shot `internal/schema/changes/*`.
  The forward schema is **already Go-owned**. So the old "port V5/V6 migrations
  into Go" work is **moot**; Phase 3 is now pure deletion of Python's bootstrap,
  not migration porting. (No migration logic is written — none is needed.)
- **Read-site drift:** all read functions still exist; `_project_root_for_task`
  is now `_main_root_for_task` (`task_cmd.py:373`); the `session_cmd.py:1300`
  task read is now `:1434`. `suggestions_cmd.py` has **no** task reads — it drops
  off the worklist.
- **`endless-event` uses raw stdlib** `switch os.Args[1]` + per-subcommand
  `flag.NewFlagSet` (no go-cliutil); first call is `monitor.ConsumeDBContextFlag()`.
  New read subcommands follow that same internal-binary style.

## The pattern (mirror the write bridge)

Writes today: `event_bridge.py` → `shutil.which("endless-event")` +
`config.require_db_context()` + `*config.go_db_context_args()` (the E-1429
`--config-dir` threading) → `subprocess.run(capture_output, text)` →
`json.loads(stdout)`. Reads mirror this exactly via a new `query_bridge.py`.

**CRITICAL:** the read bridge MUST inject `*config.go_db_context_args()` right
after the binary name, exactly as `event_bridge.py:135-137` does, or reads trip
the E-1429 worktree DB-context gate (`monitor.guardWorktreeDBContext`).

**JSON I/O convention:**
- Single-row query → one JSON object on stdout, or literal `null` if not found.
- List query → JSON array, or `[]` if empty.
- Error → stderr prefixed `endless-event: error:`, exit 1 (exit 2 for missing
  required flags).
- Output shapes must carry **every field** Python's default / `--json` / `--llm`
  formatters consume today (capture per-command during Phase 1).

**Phase 1 subcommand surface:**

| Subcommand | Replaces (Python) | Output |
|---|---|---|
| `get-task --id N` | `detail_item` core query | object/null |
| `list-tasks [filters]` | `show_plan` | array |
| `next-tasks [filters --limit N]` | `next_tasks` | array |
| `search-tasks --query S [...]` | `search_tasks` | array |
| `task-children --id N` | `detail_item --children` | array |
| `task-relations --id N [--rel-type T]` | `get_all_relations` / `_related_task_ids` / `show_relations` | array |
| `active-tasks` | `active_tasks` | array |
| `recent-tasks [--limit N]` | `recent_tasks` | array |
| `list-decisions [filters]` | `list_decisions` | array |
| `task-by-source-file --path P` | `_main_root_for_task` SQL | object/null |

Filters mirror the Click flags: `--phase --status --tier --parent --related-to
--rel-type --all --sort --limit`.

## DRY sources (lift, don't rewrite)

- `internal/web/queries.go`: `GetProjectTasks` (≈ `show_plan`: tree + child_count
  + blocked_by GROUP_CONCAT), `GetCurrentWork` (≈ `active_tasks`),
  `GetProjectDependencies` (≈ `get_all_relations`), plus group/detail helpers.
  These bind to web `data.*` view structs — extract the SQL/scan into a shared
  read helper, then have web reuse it.
- `internal/monitor/task.go`: `GetActiveTasks`, `TaskText`, `GetProjectName`.
- Go DB handle: `monitor.DB()` (`internal/monitor/db.go:252`); path honors the
  E-1429 dbContextDir then XDG.

## Phases (each is a child task; gate on prior phase landing; own worktree each)

1. **Add Go read subcommands to `endless-event`** (child task). Go read helpers +
   the 10 subcommands above; lift overlapping SQL from `web/queries.go`; per-helper
   tests. No Python change.
2. **Add `query_bridge.py` + cut every task read over** (child, blocked by P1).
   New bridge mirroring `event_bridge.py` (incl. `--config-dir`); replace each
   `db.query` task read; delete the dead Python SQL bodies; golden-output parity.
3. **Remove Python's schema bootstrap** (child, blocked by P2). Delete
   `_init_schema`/`_migrate*` from `db.py`; Go owns DB creation; subsumes the
   E-1116 empty-DB crash. **Scope note:** db.py's `query()` stays — Python still
   reads non-task tables (sessions/projects/suggestions); those are out of scope.
   "Python has zero SQLite knowledge" applies to **tasks**, not all tables.

## Out of scope

- Web/TUI reads (already Go). P1 lifts shared SQL so web reuses it; no UI cutover.
- Non-task table reads (sessions/projects/suggestions) — separate future tasks.
- Goal/focus subsystem — entirely unbuilt on main; E-894 has zero dependency.
- Any migration logic (none needed; forward schema already in `schema.sql`).

## Decision log

- 2026-05-26 (Mike): **full decouple, Phases 1-3** — the schema phase is cheap
  now that E-1459 moved forward schema to Go; do it rather than leave Python a
  SQLite foothold.
- 2026-05-26 (Mike): **start fresh, no salvage** of the stranded `e-1031` pilot.
- 2026-05-26: reconcile-then-spawn; this session does DB-only planning (no claim,
  no worktree); implementation spawned per-phase.

payload.title: Add Go read subcommands to endless-event for task-display queries
payload.description: Add Go read helpers + endless-event subcommands (get-task, list-tasks, next-tasks, search-tasks, task-children, task-relations, active-tasks, recent-tasks, list-decisions, task-by-source-file) that return task-display data as JSON, mirroring the existing endless-event switch/flag dispatch. Lift overlapping SQL from internal/web/queries.go (DRY). No Python changes this phase. Phase 1 of E-894 full-decouple.
payload.text:
# E-894 Phase 1 — Go read subcommands in endless-event

Umbrella context: `endless task show E-894 --text`.

## Goal

Add Go-side read helpers and `endless-event` subcommands that return task-display
data as JSON. **No Python changes in this phase** — Python still reads SQLite
until Phase 2 cuts over. This phase ends with a working, tested Go read surface.

## Build

Mirror the existing `endless-event` style (current `main`):
- Dispatch: add `case "<name>":` to the `switch os.Args[1]` in
  `cmd/endless-event/main.go` (first stmt is already `monitor.ConsumeDBContextFlag()`).
- Each subcommand: own `flag.NewFlagSet(name, flag.ExitOnError)`, parse
  `os.Args[2:]`, run helper, `json.Marshal` to stdout. Raw stdlib flag (this is
  an internal bridge binary, not a user-facing CLI — go-cliutil not warranted).
- DB handle via `monitor.DB()`.
- New Go code: ClearPath, doterr, go-dt for paths; go-sqlparams if building
  dynamic WHERE/sort/limit.

Subcommands (replace the named Python query; see umbrella table for the full list):
`get-task`, `list-tasks`, `next-tasks`, `search-tasks`, `task-children`,
`task-relations`, `active-tasks`, `recent-tasks`, `list-decisions`,
`task-by-source-file`.

Helpers: put the read helpers where current Go task reads live (`internal/monitor/task.go`
already has `GetActiveTasks`/`TaskText`/`GetProjectName`) or a sibling
`task_reads.go` if it grows. **Lift overlapping SQL from `internal/web/queries.go`**
(`GetProjectTasks` ≈ list-tasks, `GetProjectDependencies` ≈ task-relations,
`GetCurrentWork` ≈ active-tasks) into the shared helper rather than duplicate;
have web call the shared helper (DRY).

## Output contract

- single-row → object or `null`; list → array or `[]`.
- error → stderr `endless-event: error: <msg>`, exit 1; missing required flag → exit 2.
- **Every field** Python's default / `--json` / `--llm` formatters read today must
  appear in the JSON. Capture the exact field set per command before coding:
  inspect each Python formatter (`detail_item`, `show_plan`, `next_tasks`,
  `search_tasks`, `active_tasks`, `recent_tasks`, `list_decisions`,
  relation rendering) for the columns/aliases it consumes.

## Filters to support (mirror Click flags)

`--phase --status --tier --parent --related-to --rel-type --all --sort --limit`,
plus `--id`, `--query`, `--path` where applicable.

## Verification

- `go test ./internal/...` — per-helper table-driven tests against a fixture DB.
- `endless-event get-task --id 894` → valid JSON object matching the captured shape.
- `endless-event list-tasks --status ready --phase now --sort tier` → expected rows/order.
- Exit codes: 0 success/empty, 1 DB error, 2 missing required flag.
- `endless-event validate-db` still passes.
- `just build` clean.

payload.title: Add query_bridge.py and cut Python task-display reads over to Go
payload.description: Add src/endless/query_bridge.py mirroring event_bridge.py (including the E-1429 --config-dir DB-context threading) and route every Python task-display read through the Phase 1 Go subcommands; delete the dead Python SQL bodies, keep formatting. Byte-for-byte output parity across all formats/filters. Phase 2 of E-894.
payload.text:
# E-894 Phase 2 — query_bridge.py + cut task reads over to Go

Umbrella context: `endless task show E-894 --text`. Blocked by Phase 1 (needs the
`endless-event` read subcommands landed on main).

## Goal

Route every Python task-display read through the Go subcommands from Phase 1, and
delete the dead Python SQL. Output must be byte-for-byte unchanged.

## Build the bridge

Create `src/endless/query_bridge.py`, a **read** mirror of `event_bridge.py`:
- `_run_query(subcmd, *args)`: `shutil.which("endless-event")` (raise the same
  "not found — just build" ClickException if missing) → build argv
  `[bin, *config.go_db_context_args(), subcmd, *args]` AFTER calling
  `config.require_db_context()` → `subprocess.run(capture_output=True, text=True)`
  → on non-zero exit raise ClickException(stderr) → `json.loads(stdout.strip())`.
- **CRITICAL:** the `*config.go_db_context_args()` (`--config-dir`) injection is
  mandatory — copy it from `event_bridge.py:135-137`. Without it, reads hit the
  E-1429 worktree DB gate. Mirror exactly.
- Wrappers (one per subcommand): `get_task`, `list_tasks`, `next_tasks`,
  `search_tasks`, `task_children`, `task_relations`, `active_tasks`,
  `recent_tasks`, `list_decisions`, `task_by_source_file`.

## Cut over each read site (current main line numbers)

`task_cmd.py`: `show_plan` (query :949), `detail_item` (:2780; children :2814/:2851/:2905),
`next_tasks` (:1111), `search_tasks` (:3267), `active_tasks` (:1297),
`recent_tasks` (:1388), `list_decisions` (:1475), `get_all_relations` (:3665),
`_related_task_ids` (:3756/:3767/:3774), `show_relations` (:3792),
`_main_root_for_task` (:373).
`session_cmd.py`: the `SELECT id,title,status FROM tasks WHERE id=?` at `:1434`.

For each: replace the `db.query(...)` body with the matching `query_bridge` call;
keep all formatting/rendering code. Delete the now-dead SQL strings.

## Verification

- Golden-output parity: for the SAME DB snapshot, `task list`, `task show E-894`,
  `task next`, `task search "migrate"`, `task active`, `task recent`,
  `task decisions`, `task relations <id>` produce identical output pre/post.
- All formats: default Rich table, `--json`, `--llm`, `--tree`, `--no-description`,
  `--text`, `--children`, `--outcome`.
- All filter combos: `--status --phase --tier --parent --related-to --rel-type
  --all --sort --limit`.
- `just test` (pytest) passes.
- `grep -rn "db.query" src/endless/ | grep -i task` → zero hits against tasks/task_deps.
- Exercise from a worktree (sandbox routing) to confirm `--config-dir` threading works.

fields.outcome: Audit completed (recorded by Mike)

fields.text: The tasks.text column has been treated as 'the plan' (planned plan-bearing detector keys off its presence), but there is no field for problem analysis, design briefs, research notes, or decision rationale — content that is long-form but not a plan. The moment plan-bearing detection lands, anything stored in text gets interpreted as 'planned'. Rename tasks.text->tasks.plan and add a separate tasks.analysis field. Constraints from E-1073 decision: analysis is pre-plan design content only, no time-frozen specifics (bytes/line numbers), MUST NOT be NULLed when the plan file fully documents the design. The new analysis column also needs a CLI surface (--analysis / --analysis-file on add/update; render in task show); the column added ahead of E-999 has no surface today.

Three rules govern the analysis field on tasks (extends E-1058's description policy and grounds E-1064's audit). (1) Analysis is pre-plan/pre-implementation design content only; once a task ships, the analysis is historical record. Post-shipping staleness is not a concern because the design is realised in code and plan files; auditing post-implementation analysis for currency is a category error. (2) Analysis must not include time-frozen specifics — exact byte counts, file sizes, line numbers, or sha hashes of moving artifacts. Such data has near-zero historical value and creates false-precision drift. Cite file or function names instead and let the reader re-locate at pickup. (3) When a task's plan file (--text/plan) fully documents the chosen design, analysis MUST NOT be NULLed; is it a historical record.

payload.title: Add sessions.goal_task_id to preserve session origin task when active focus shifts
payload.description: active_task_id is the only session-task pointer today; claim and bind overwrite it, so a session that pivots to a follow-up loses its origin task from the tmux status bar and any cold-return orientation. Add a nullable, write-once goal_task_id column populated by claim (both fields) and optionally by bind --include-goal. tmux status renders [E-NNNN] when goal equals or is null relative to active; [E-GGGG/E-AAAA] when they diverge.

fields.text:
# Context

Sessions last for days. `sessions.active_task_id` is the only session→task pointer today, and both `endless task claim` and `endless task bind` overwrite it. When a session pivots to a follow-up (a `cleans_up` child filed mid-stream, an in-session-discovered fix), the origin task is lost from the tmux status bar and from any cold-return orientation. Returning to a multi-day session and seeing `[E-1402]` in the status while the conversation is about E-1479 is exactly the failure mode this addresses.

Reading back through the conversation log as the recall mechanism is unworkable in practice — too slow, too exhausting. The status bar needs to carry both pieces of state.

# Decided constraints

These were decided in chat with Mike before the spawn. Do not re-litigate.

- **New column**: `sessions.goal_task_id INTEGER`, nullable, foreign key to `tasks(id)` ON DELETE SET NULL.
- **Write-once after first set**: once `goal_task_id` is non-NULL for a session, it cannot be changed. Nullable initial state is the only way a session can have it set later.
- **`endless task claim` writes both**: sets `goal_task_id` AND `active_task_id` to the claimed task (claim is the natural "this session is here for X" entry point).
- **`endless task bind` writes only `active_task_id` by default**: bind is the lightweight "focus shift" verb; preserving goal is the point.
- **`endless task bind --include-goal`**: also writes `goal_task_id` when it is currently NULL. Use case: session was started without `claim` (no goal recorded), user wants to set the goal after the fact. Refuses (or no-ops with a notice — see open question below) when goal is already set, because of the write-once invariant.
- **Status-line render**:
  - When `goal_task_id IS NULL` OR `goal_task_id = active_task_id`: render `[E-NNNN]` (today's behavior).
  - When they differ: render `[E-GGGG/E-AAAA]` — goal in light gray, active in bright yellow.

# Open sub-questions (to resolve with Mike in plan mode)

1. **Release semantics.** `endless task release` clears `active_task_id` today. Does it also clear `goal_task_id`, leave it alone, or only clear it via an explicit `--include-goal` symmetric to bind? Argument for leaving alone: the session's origin doesn't change when focus is released. Argument for clearing: a fully-released session shouldn't carry a goal pointer to nothing.
2. **Immutability enforcement layer.** Options: (a) DB-level CHECK constraint or BEFORE UPDATE trigger that refuses a non-NULL → different-non-NULL transition; (b) Python guard in `claim_item` / `bind_item`; (c) both. House preference is gates not guardrails — DB-level is stronger.
3. **Event payload.** `task.claimed` carries `session_id` today (payload field). Does the projector need a separate event kind (e.g. `session.goal_set`), or does `task.claimed` grow a marker indicating whether goal should also be written, or does the projector compute goal-write from the session row's current state (set if NULL, leave alone otherwise)? The third option keeps payloads stable but moves logic into the projector.
4. **`bind --include-goal` on an already-set goal.** Hard error (refuse, exit 1), or warning + no-op (write proceeds with active only and prints "goal already set, leaving as E-NNNN")?
5. **tmux color codes.** What exact ANSI escapes for "light gray" goal and "bright yellow" active? Match whatever the current status renderer is doing for the existing single-task display and apply a dim modifier to goal.

# Files to modify

- `internal/schema/schema.sql` — add `goal_task_id` column + index if useful for lookups.
- `src/endless/task_cmd.py` — `claim_item` writes goal_task_id alongside active_task_id; `bind_item` accepts `--include-goal` and writes goal only when currently NULL.
- `src/endless/cli.py` — `task bind` Click command gains `--include-goal` flag.
- `internal/events/payload.go` — depending on Open Q3, possibly a new field on `TaskClaimedPayload` or a new event kind.
- `internal/events/projector.go` — handler that writes goal_task_id (see Open Q3).
- Wherever the tmux status-line renderer lives — likely under `cmd/endless-tmux/` or `internal/tmux/`. Add the diverged-display branch.
- `tests/test_task_claim_worktree.py` — verify `claim` writes both.
- `tests/test_task_release.py` — verify release behavior (per Open Q1 resolution).
- New test file for `bind --include-goal` if not already covered.
- Go tests for the status renderer's diverged branch.

# Out of scope

- The existing `active_goal_id`, `primary_focus_id`, `current_focus_id` columns on `sessions`. Those belong to a stalled task-set Mike intends to re-imagine; do not touch them.
- Backfilling `goal_task_id` for existing session rows (per "no migration logic for unshipped software"). New sessions get the column populated on first claim; old sessions stay NULL.

# Verification

End-to-end manual tests (run after implementation lands):

1. **Fresh session, normal claim**: spawn a new Claude session, run `endless task claim E-XXXX`. Expect tmux status `[E-XXXX]`; expect `sessions.goal_task_id` and `sessions.active_task_id` both equal to the task's id.
2. **Session pivots via bind**: in an already-claimed session, run `endless task bind E-YYYY`. Expect tmux status `[E-XXXX/E-YYYY]` (goal gray, active yellow); expect `goal_task_id` unchanged, `active_task_id = YYYY`.
3. **Late goal-set**: session started without claim (goal_task_id IS NULL), run `endless task bind E-ZZZZ --include-goal`. Expect both columns set to ZZZZ; expect status `[E-ZZZZ]`.
4. **Write-once invariant**: in session from test 1, run `endless task bind E-WWWW --include-goal`. Expect the resolution from Open Q4 (refuse or warn-and-proceed).
5. **Release behavior**: run `endless task release` in test 1's session. Expect behavior per Open Q1 resolution.

Automated:
- Python: extend `tests/test_task_claim_worktree.py` to assert both columns after claim; new tests for bind --include-goal paths.
- Go: status renderer test for the diverged-display branch with both pane variants.
- `just test` (Python) and `just test-go` (Go) both green.

payload.title: Remove Python's SQLite schema bootstrap so Go owns DB creation
payload.description: Remove Python's schema bootstrap (_init_schema/_migrate* in src/endless/db.py) so Go's schema.sql (applied by monitor.DB()) is the sole owner of DB creation; subsumes the E-1116 empty-DB crash. db.py's query() stays for remaining non-task reads (sessions/projects/suggestions) — out of scope here. No migration logic written (forward schema already in schema.sql per E-1459). Phase 3 of E-894.
payload.text:
# E-894 Phase 3 — Remove Python's SQLite schema bootstrap

Umbrella context: `endless task show E-894 --text`. Blocked by Phase 2.

## Goal

Stop Python from creating or migrating the DB schema; Go (`schema.sql` applied by
`monitor.DB()`) becomes the sole owner of DB creation. Subsumes the E-1116
empty-DB crash. **No migration logic is written** — the forward schema already
lives in `internal/schema/schema.sql` (E-1459); there is nothing to port.

## Scope boundary (important)

This phase removes the schema **bootstrap**, NOT all Python SQLite access.
After Phase 2, Python no longer reads *task* tables — but Python still reads
non-task tables (sessions, projects, suggestions, channels) via `db.query`.
So `db.py`'s `query()`/connection helpers **stay**; only the schema-management
code goes. "Python has zero SQLite knowledge" is true for **tasks**, the stated
E-894 goal — not yet for every table (those are separate future tasks).

## Build (subtractive)

In `src/endless/db.py`:
- Remove `_init_schema` (`:51`) and `_migrate` + `_migrate_v2`..`_migrate_v6`
  (`:92`, `:418` V5 task_deps, `:522` V6 outcome). Remove the relative-path
  `schema.sql` execution hack (`:15`, the "temporary until E-894" comment).
- `get_db()` no longer calls `_init_schema`/`_migrate`; it just opens the existing
  DB connection for the remaining non-task reads.

## Open design point — DB-creation ordering (resolve at implementation)

Most CLI commands are Python entry points. Once Python stops running
`_init_schema`, a brand-new DB must be created by Go **before** any Python
`db.query` opens it (else "no such table"). Options to evaluate:
- Have `db.get_db()` ensure-DB via a cheap Go call (e.g. `endless-event` opens
  `monitor.DB()` which applies `schema.sql`) when the DB file is missing/empty.
- Or guarantee a Go path runs first on fresh install (setup/install flow).
Pick the simplest that keeps a cold `~/.config/endless/` working end-to-end.
Do NOT add backward-compat shims (unshipped software, single user).

## Verification

- Fresh/empty `~/.config/endless/` (or E-1078 sandbox fixture): first command
  creates the DB via Go alone; E-1116's `OperationalError: no such table: tasks`
  no longer reproduces.
- `grep -rn "_migrate\|_init_schema\|user_version" src/endless/` → zero.
- `pytest` passes against an empty config dir.
- Full smoke: `just install`, cold config dir, run `task add` / `task show` /
  `task list` / `task next` / `task search` / `task confirm` / `session` flow.
- Mark E-1116 resolved (this subsumes it).

fields.text:
# E-894 — Move task-display DB reads from Python to Go

> Refreshed 2026-05-26 against current `main` (HEAD survey). Supersedes the
> 2026-05-02 plan, whose "focus pilot" template and `migrate.go` framework
> assumptions are stale (see Reconciliation below).

## Goal

Move every task-display DB **read** out of Python and into Go, so Python has
zero SQLite knowledge for task operations. Python keeps rendering (Rich tables,
markdown, tree, `--json`, `--llm`); only the data-fetch step moves. Decoupling
scope: **full** (Phases 1-3) per decision with Mike 2026-05-26 — also retire
Python's schema bootstrap so Go owns DB creation.

## Reconciliation with current main (what changed since 2026-05-02)

- **No "focus pilot" to mirror.** The old plan said it would copy an established
  `query_bridge.py` + `internal/events/query.go` + `endless-event get-focus`
  read pattern. That code was never landed (stranded uncommitted in an orphaned
  `e-1031` worktree) and is abandoned. We build the read pattern **fresh**,
  mirroring the **write** bridge (`event_bridge.py`) instead. E-1053, which
  formalized that pilot, is obsoleted.
- **Schema framework is gone.** E-1459 deleted the E-863 `migrate.go` versioned
  framework and replaced it with `internal/schema/schema.sql` (authoritative,
  applied by Go on every `monitor.DB()` open) + one-shot `internal/schema/changes/*`.
  The forward schema is **already Go-owned**. So the old "port V5/V6 migrations
  into Go" work is **moot**; Phase 3 is now pure deletion of Python's bootstrap,
  not migration porting. (No migration logic is written — none is needed.)
- **Read-site drift:** all read functions still exist; `_project_root_for_task`
  is now `_main_root_for_task` (`task_cmd.py:373`); the `session_cmd.py:1300`
  task read is now `:1434`. `suggestions_cmd.py` has **no** task reads — it drops
  off the worklist.
- **`endless-event` uses raw stdlib** `switch os.Args[1]` + per-subcommand
  `flag.NewFlagSet` (no go-cliutil); first call is `monitor.ConsumeDBContextFlag()`.
  New read subcommands follow that same internal-binary style.

## The pattern (mirror the write bridge)

Writes today: `event_bridge.py` → `shutil.which("endless-event")` +
`config.require_db_context()` + `*config.go_db_context_args()` (the E-1429
`--config-dir` threading) → `subprocess.run(capture_output, text)` →
`json.loads(stdout)`. Reads mirror this exactly via a new `query_bridge.py`.

**CRITICAL:** the read bridge MUST inject `*config.go_db_context_args()` right
after the binary name, exactly as `event_bridge.py:135-137` does, or reads trip
the E-1429 worktree DB-context gate (`monitor.guardWorktreeDBContext`).

**JSON I/O convention:**
- Single-row query → one JSON object on stdout, or literal `null` if not found.
- List query → JSON array, or `[]` if empty.
- Error → stderr prefixed `endless-event: error:`, exit 1 (exit 2 for missing
  required flags).
- Output shapes must carry **every field** Python's default / `--json` / `--llm`
  formatters consume today (capture per-command during Phase 1).

**Phase 1 subcommand surface:**

| Subcommand | Replaces (Python) | Output |
|---|---|---|
| `get-task --id N` | `detail_item` core query | object/null |
| `list-tasks [filters]` | `show_plan` | array |
| `next-tasks [filters --limit N]` | `next_tasks` | array |
| `search-tasks --query S [...]` | `search_tasks` | array |
| `task-children --id N` | `detail_item --children` | array |
| `task-relations --id N [--rel-type T]` | `get_all_relations` / `_related_task_ids` / `show_relations` | array |
| `active-tasks` | `active_tasks` | array |
| `recent-tasks [--limit N]` | `recent_tasks` | array |
| `list-decisions [filters]` | `list_decisions` | array |
| `task-by-source-file --path P` | `_main_root_for_task` SQL | object/null |

Filters mirror the Click flags: `--phase --status --tier --parent --related-to
--rel-type --all --sort --limit`.

## DRY sources (lift, don't rewrite)

- `internal/web/queries.go`: `GetProjectTasks` (≈ `show_plan`: tree + child_count
  + blocked_by GROUP_CONCAT), `GetCurrentWork` (≈ `active_tasks`),
  `GetProjectDependencies` (≈ `get_all_relations`), plus group/detail helpers.
  These bind to web `data.*` view structs — extract the SQL/scan into a shared
  read helper, then have web reuse it.
- `internal/monitor/task.go`: `GetActiveTasks`, `TaskText`, `GetProjectName`.
- Go DB handle: `monitor.DB()` (`internal/monitor/db.go:252`); path honors the
  E-1429 dbContextDir then XDG.

## Phases (each is a child task; gate on prior phase landing; own worktree each)

1. **Add Go read subcommands to `endless-event`** (child task). Go read helpers +
   the 10 subcommands above; lift overlapping SQL from `web/queries.go`; per-helper
   tests. No Python change.
2. **Add `query_bridge.py` + cut every task read over** (child, blocked by P1).
   New bridge mirroring `event_bridge.py` (incl. `--config-dir`); replace each
   `db.query` task read; delete the dead Python SQL bodies; golden-output parity.
3. **Remove Python's schema bootstrap** (child, blocked by P2). Delete
   `_init_schema`/`_migrate*` from `db.py`; Go owns DB creation; subsumes the
   E-1116 empty-DB crash. **Scope note:** db.py's `query()` stays — Python still
   reads non-task tables (sessions/projects/suggestions); those are out of scope.
   "Python has zero SQLite knowledge" applies to **tasks**, not all tables.

## Out of scope

- Web/TUI reads (already Go). P1 lifts shared SQL so web reuses it; no UI cutover.
- Non-task table reads (sessions/projects/suggestions) — separate future tasks.
- Goal/focus subsystem — entirely unbuilt on main; E-894 has zero dependency.
- Any migration logic (none needed; forward schema already in `schema.sql`).

## Decision log

- 2026-05-26 (Mike): **full decouple, Phases 1-3** — the schema phase is cheap
  now that E-1459 moved forward schema to Go; do it rather than leave Python a
  SQLite foothold.
- 2026-05-26 (Mike): **start fresh, no salvage** of the stranded `e-1031` pilot.
- 2026-05-26: reconcile-then-spawn; this session does DB-only planning (no claim,
  no worktree); implementation spawned per-phase.

## Closeout (E-894 has no code of its own)

E-894 is the umbrella; all implementation lives in the phase children. Endless
has **no automatic parent rollup** — confirming the children does NOT flip the
parent. So once **E-1481, E-1482, and E-1484 are all confirmed**, set
**E-894 → verify** (`endless task update E-894 --status verify`); Mike confirms
E-894. The terminal phase (E-1484) carries the same reminder so the spawned
session that finishes the chain does the flip.

# E-894 Phase 3 — Remove Python's SQLite schema bootstrap

Umbrella context: `endless task show E-894 --text`. Blocked by Phase 2.

## Goal

## Build (subtractive)

In `src/endless/db.py`:
- Remove `_init_schema` (`:51`) and `_migrate` + `_migrate_v2`..`_migrate_v6`
  (`:92`, `:418` V5 task_deps, `:522` V6 outcome). Remove the relative-path
  `schema.sql` execution hack (`:15`, the "temporary until E-894" comment).
- `get_db()` no longer calls `_init_schema`/`_migrate`; it just opens the existing
  DB connection for the remaining non-task reads.

## Open design point — DB-creation ordering (resolve at implementation)

Most CLI commands are Python entry points. Once Python stops running
