`db.query` opens it (else "no such table"). Options to evaluate:
- Have `db.get_db()` ensure-DB via a cheap Go call (e.g. `endless-event` opens
  `monitor.DB()` which applies `schema.sql`) when the DB file is missing/empty.
- Or guarantee a Go path runs first on fresh install (setup/install flow).
Pick the simplest that keeps a cold `~/.config/endless/` working end-to-end.
Do NOT add backward-compat shims (unshipped software, single user).

## Verification

- Fresh/empty `~/.config/endless/` (or E-1078 sandbox fixture): first command
  creates the DB via Go alone; E-1116's `OperationalError: no such table: tasks`
  no longer reproduces.
- `grep -rn "_migrate\|_init_schema\|user_version" src/endless/` → zero.
- `pytest` passes against an empty config dir.
- Full smoke: `just install`, cold config dir, run `task add` / `task show` /
  `task list` / `task next` / `task search` / `task confirm` / `session` flow.
- Mark E-1116 resolved (this subsumes it).

## Closeout — flip the umbrella (no auto-rollup)

E-1484 is the **terminal phase** of E-894, and Endless does not roll parent
status up from children. After this phase lands AND E-1481 + E-1482 are
confirmed, set the umbrella: `endless task update E-894 --status verify`
(Mike then confirms E-894). Don't leave E-894 stuck in `ready` once all three
phases are done.

fields.outcome: Obsolete: start_item no longer exists — E-1232 renamed 'task start' to 'task claim'; the command is now a hidden deprecated stub (cli.py:1246), so there is nothing to port. claim_item's residual Python task read (task_cmd.py:2372) and all other non-task Python SQL belong to a broader 'port all Python DB access to Go' effort being scoped, not this start-specific ticket.

payload.title: Surface closeable parent tasks in the per-project next list
payload.description: Detect parent tasks whose children are all in terminal status (confirmed/assumed/obsolete/declined) while the parent itself is non-terminal, and auto-surface them in E-1421's curated per-project 'next' list (e.g. a pending-triage-style bucket) so the user sees 'ready to close' parents and can flip them to verify/confirm. Replaces the stopgap of burying closeout notes in plan text (as done for E-894/E-1484). Builds on E-1421's consistency-hook + render machinery (confirmed). Open design point for the plan: a hook that writes closeable parents into the list vs a render-side computation (mirroring E-1421's race-free terminal-status filter).

payload.title: Remove all Python SQLite access by porting DB reads and writes to Go
payload.description: Umbrella for the 'Python has zero SQLite knowledge' milestone: port every DB read AND write, for every table, out of Python and into Go (endless-event subcommands / event pipeline), keeping Click + rendering in Python. The missing middle between E-894 (task-display reads only) and E-1063 (port the entire CLI incl. rendering, deferred). Most residual Python SQL is direct writes (38 sites) bypassing the event log; writes are classified per-subsystem (durable domain data routed through emit_event for rebuild-fidelity; local/transient via Go write helpers). Decomposes into per-subsystem children G1-G8 (planned at pickup).
payload.text:
# Remove all Python SQLite access (port DB access to Go)

## Goal

Python has **zero SQLite knowledge**: every DB **read and write**, for every
table, moves to Go (via `endless-event` subcommands and/or the event pipeline).
Python keeps its Click commands and rendering. This is the "no Python SQLite"
milestone — distinct from its two neighbors:

- **E-894** — task *display reads* only (the first slice; stays under E-799, linked here).
- **E-1063** — port the *entire* Python CLI (incl. rendering) to Go — broader,
  explicitly deferred ("Not now… only on a forcing function"). This epic is the
  DB-access-only subset, achievable without committing to the full CLI port.

## Writes decision (Mike, 2026-05-26): classify per-subsystem

Most residual Python SQL is **direct writes** (38 `db.execute` sites) that bypass
the event log, so `rebuild-db` (E-807) can't reproduce them. At each child's plan
time, classify every table it touches:

- **Durable domain data** (projects, notes, suggestions, channels) → route writes
  through `emit_event` → Go, so they're **event-sourced and rebuild-faithful**.
  This fixes a latent integrity gap.
- **Local / transient state** (session transcript offsets/paths, recap flags, etc.)
  → Go **write helper**, deliberately NOT in the event log.

## Established pattern (per child)

Mirror E-894's two-phase shape: (1) Go read helpers + `endless-event` read
subcommands; (2) Python cutover via a `query_bridge` wrapper, deleting the dead
read SQL. Plus writes per the classification above. **Reuse existing Go** where it
exists: `internal/web/queries.go` (reads), `internal/monitor/*.go`, and the
existing Go writers for `channels` (MCP) and `suggestions`.

## Children (G1–G8) — filed as needs_plan; plan each at pickup

- **G1 Projects** (`projects`, `project_deps`) — largest fan-out; the copy-pasted
  `_resolve_project` "SELECT name FROM projects WHERE path=?" helper lives in ~6
  files — consolidate first. `reconcile` is a real FS↔DB sync engine.
- **G2 Sessions** (`sessions`, `session_messages`, `session_messages_fts`) —
  biggest single-file effort; transcript/recap/resolution engine; 13 direct writes.
- **G3 Channels** (`conversations`, `messages`, `channels`) — `channels` already
  has a Go writer (MCP registration); reuse, don't fork.
- **G4 Notes** (`notes`) — small; also written/deleted by `unregister` (G1).
- **G5 Suggestions** — `accept` path does `INSERT INTO tasks` **directly**,
  bypassing event sourcing; route it through `emit_event` (write-correctness, not
  just a read port). Suggestions are *created* by Go already.
- **G6 Task non-display** — the tail of `task_cmd.py` E-1482 doesn't cover:
  session-resolution reads, ownership checks, sort-order, worktree/root lookups,
  and `link`/`unlink`/`move`/`start_chat` direct writes. **Blocked by E-1482.**
- **G7 Docs** — the `documents` table is **DROPPED in migration v2**; verify
  `endless docs` is dead, then likely just delete the command. Don't port blindly.
- **G8 `endless sql` + db.py teardown** — **TERMINAL; blocked by G1–G7.** Port the
  `endless sql` escape hatch (`cli.py:469`) to a Go subcommand, then delete db.py's
  `query`/`scalar`/`exists`/`execute` and — once `endless sql` is ported —
  `get_db()` + `import sqlite3`. That is the moment Python hits zero SQLite.

## End state

db.py read/write helpers deleted once all groups port. `get_db()` + `import
sqlite3` are deletable iff `endless sql` becomes a Go subcommand (G8). Schema
creation is already Go-owned (E-1459 / E-1484), so no migration logic is written.

## Relations

relates_to E-799 (event-sourced architecture — the durable-write classification
ties directly to it), E-1063 (the broader full-CLI-port umbrella — this is its
DB-only subset), E-894 (the task-display slice + the pioneer two-phase pattern).

payload.title: Port project DB access to Go (projects, project_deps)
payload.description: Port all Python reads and writes for projects/project_deps to Go: register/unregister/rename/set/scan/reconcile/status/list/discover/resolve, plus the copy-pasted _resolve_project 'SELECT name FROM projects WHERE path=?' helper (consolidate first). Writes per E-1486 classification (projects are durable → route through emit_event). G1 of E-1486.

payload.title: Port session DB access to Go (sessions, session_messages)
payload.description: Port Python reads and writes for sessions/session_messages/session_messages_fts to Go: history/list/search/recap/resolution/transcript-import (session_cmd.py + session_activity.py); 13 direct writes today. Per E-1486: transcript offsets/paths/recap flags are local/transient (Go write helper, not event log); durable session facts via emit_event. G2 of E-1486.

payload.title: Port channel DB access to Go (conversations, messages, channels)
payload.description: Port channel_cmd Python reads and writes (beacon/connect/send/inbox/close/list) over conversations/messages/channels to Go. channels already has a Go writer (MCP registration) — reuse, don't fork. Writes per E-1486 classification. G3 of E-1486.

payload.title: Port notes DB access to Go
payload.description: Port Python reads and writes for the notes table (notes_cmd add/list/resolve; deletes in unregister) to Go. Notes are durable domain data -> writes via emit_event per E-1486. Coordinate with G1 (project-scoped). G4 of E-1486.

payload.title: Port suggestions DB access to Go and route accept through events
payload.description: Port suggestions_cmd reads to Go and fix the accept path, which currently does INSERT INTO tasks directly — route task creation through emit_event like all other task creation (write-correctness). Suggestions are created by Go already. G5 of E-1486.

payload.title: Port remaining task_cmd non-display DB access to Go
payload.description: Port the task_cmd.py DB access E-1482 doesn't cover: session-resolution reads, ownership checks, sort-order, worktree/main-root lookups, and link/unlink/move/start_chat direct writes (relation/reparent writes should become emit_event). Blocked by E-1482 (task display read path). G6 of E-1486.

payload.title: Remove or port the docs command's documents-table access
payload.description: The documents table is DROPPED in migration v2, so 'endless docs' (docs_cmd.py) likely errors/returns empty on any migrated DB. Verify whether the command is dead; if so delete it rather than port; otherwise port its reads to Go. G7 of E-1486.

payload.title: Port the endless sql escape hatch to Go and delete db.py SQLite helpers
payload.description: Terminal step of E-1486. Port the 'endless sql' escape hatch (cli.py:469) to a Go subcommand, then delete db.py's query/scalar/exists/execute and — once endless sql no longer needs in-process SQLite — get_db() and 'import sqlite3', so Python hits zero SQLite. Blocked by all other groups (G1-G7). G8 of E-1486.

fields.text:
# E-1479 — Add `--record-only` flag to `endless worktree land`

## Context

`task_landings` is the projection of `task.landed` events emitted by `endless worktree land`. When a land happened outside that flow — manually (`git merge`), via tooling experiments, or before the `task_landings` table existed (E-1402, 2026-05-17) — there is no sanctioned path to register the landing after the fact. Going direct-INSERT bypasses the event-sourced contract and leaves the ledger silent about a real land.

Add `--record-only` so the existing `land` verb can emit a `task.landed` event for an already-completed land while skipping all the rebase / dedup / orphan-drop / fast-forward work. First use case: the missing `task_landings` row for E-1402.

The flag was preferred over a sibling verb (`worktree record-landing`) for two reasons: two-segment verbs are a CLI smell in this project; and a flag on the existing verb is more discoverable via `land --help` than a sibling verb someone has to know exists. The "only" carries the "skip the work" semantic clearly enough.

## Decisions

### Argument auto-discovery

When `--record-only` is supplied with no other flags, derive the missing values from the local git state.

Cascade for `branch` and `sha`:

1. **Primary** — `git for-each-ref --format='%(refname:short) %(objectname)' refs/heads/task/<id>-*` produces (branch, tip_sha) tuples. Filter out tuples whose tip_sha is already in `task_landings` for this task.
   - Exactly one remaining → use its `%(refname:short)` value (e.g. `task/1402-strengthen-session-resolver-handle-multi`, the ref name with the `refs/heads/` prefix stripped) as `branch` and its tip as `sha`.
   - Multiple remaining → refuse with a message listing the (branch, sha) candidates; require explicit `--branch` and/or `--sha`.
   - Zero remaining (none matched, or every match was already recorded) → fall through to (2).
2. **Fallback** — `git log <base-branch> --grep '^E-<id>:'`, filter out shas already present in `task_landings` for this task, pick the most-recent of the remaining candidates as `sha`. `branch` defaults to NULL because we can't reconstruct it accurately once the ref is gone.
3. **Hard error** — neither path yields a candidate.

Cascade for `landed_at`: chosen commit's committer date (the commit's `%cI` in ISO-8601 with timezone).

Cascade for `session_id`: the resolver (current session) populates `actor.session_id` on the emitted event, projector copies it into `task_landings.session_id`.

### Required flags

None. `--record-only` is a bare flag; everything else has a default.

### Override flags

- `--sha <hash>` — overrides auto-discovered sha. Skips the cascade entirely if supplied.
- `--branch <name>` — overrides auto-discovered branch. Accepts NULL via the empty string (`--branch ''`) so the user can deliberately record NULL when the ref is gone and they don't want a fabricated value.
- `--at <iso>` — overrides commit-date-derived `landed_at`. ISO-8601 with second precision, optional timezone offset (parser uses Python's `datetime.fromisoformat(ts.replace('Z', '+00:00'))`, matching `src/endless/session_cmd.py:976`).
- `--session-id <id-or-uuid>` — overrides resolver. Accepts the integer PK (`sessions.id`) or the Claude UUID (`sessions.session_id`). All-digits → `id` lookup; otherwise → `session_id` lookup. Lookup miss → hard error listing the rejected value.

### Combinations

- `--record-only --dry-run` is allowed: prints what `--record-only` would emit (task_id, branch, sha, landed_at, session_id, source = `auto-discovered`/`override`) without emitting the event. Useful occasional check.
- `--branch` / `--sha` / `--at` / `--session-id` supplied without `--record-only` → hard error: those flags only apply in record-only mode.

### Idempotency

Refuse on duplicate `(task_id, sha)` already in `task_landings`. Error names the existing row's `id` and `landed_at`. Re-landings (post-land bug fix on the same branch advancing the sha) still work because the sha differs.

### How `--at` reaches `task_landings.landed_at`

`endless-event` Go binary stamps the event envelope's `ts` from `clock.Now()` (`cmd/endless-event/main.go:114`); there is no caller override today. Add a `--ts` flag to the binary so callers can supply an ISO timestamp that overrides `clock.Now()` for the single event. Python's `emit_event` grows a pass-through `ts` parameter, which `_record_landing`'s call site (the record-only variant) supplies when the user specified `--at` (or when auto-discovery derived a commit date).

The projector continues to read `landed_at` from `evt.TS` only — single source of truth, no payload-schema divergence.

### Success output

One line on success:

```
• Recorded landing of E-NNNN (branch <name-or-(null)>, sha <short>) at <landed_at>
```

Identifiers stay on one physical line for copy-paste integrity.

### Help text

`--record-only`'s `--help` entry says "Register a task.landed event for a land that already happened; skip rebase/ff-merge. Auto-discovers branch/sha/landed_at from local git; override with --branch/--sha/--at if needed."

## Files to modify

- `src/endless/cli.py` — `worktree_land` Click command gains `--record-only`, `--sha`, `--branch`, `--at`, `--session-id` options. Validates that the four supporting flags only appear with `--record-only`.
- `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py` — `land_worktree` branches early on `record_only`: runs the auto-discovery cascade, validates the duplicate guard, calls `_record_landing` with the resolved values via a new path that doesn't require an actual worktree on disk. Existing normal flow unchanged.
- `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py` — extend `_record_landing` or add a sibling helper that accepts an optional `ts` to pass through to `emit_event`.
- `src/endless/event_bridge.py` — `emit_event` accepts optional `ts` parameter (ISO string); when present, passes `--ts <ts>` to the `endless-event` subprocess.
- `cmd/endless-event/main.go` — add `--ts <iso>` flag; when present, parse and use instead of `clock.Now()`.
- `tests/test_worktree_land_record_only.py` (new file) — see Verification below for coverage.
- `tests/test_event_bridge.py` — add regression that omitting `ts` preserves today's `clock.Now()` behavior, and that supplied `ts` flows through to the subprocess invocation.

## Out of scope

- Backfilling other historical `task_landings` rows beyond E-1402. Future bulk backfill needs would file a separate task.
- Touching the normal `land` flow's behavior. `--record-only` is strictly additive.
- Renaming or restructuring `task_landings`.
- Adding `--record-only` to commands other than `worktree land`.

## Verification

End-to-end:

1. **The E-1402 backfill itself.** After implementation lands:
   ```
   endless worktree land E-1402 --record-only --sha 33a2ea48ef1a2cfefdd9d5d86e1eef5a410434b5
   ```
   Then:
   ```
   endless sql "SELECT * FROM task_landings WHERE task_id = 1402"
   ```
   Expect one row with `sha = 33a2ea48ef1a2cfefdd9d5d86e1eef5a410434b5`, `branch IS NULL`, `landed_at = 2026-05-17T22:48:59` (the committer date of `33a2ea4`), `session_id = 461` (this session).

2. **Live land + later --record-only retry.** Land a fresh test task normally. Confirm one row. Then run `endless worktree land E-test --record-only` against the same task without `--sha`. Auto-discovery should find the (now-existing) row's sha already recorded and refuse with the duplicate-guard error.

3. **Auto-discovery happy path.** Pick a task whose `task/<id>-*` branch ref is still alive but never landed (e.g., a test branch). Run `endless worktree land E-test --record-only`. Auto-discovery resolves both branch and sha from the ref; row lands with correct values.

4. **Auto-discovery fallback path.** Pick a task whose branch ref has been reaped but whose subject-grep matches. Run `endless worktree land E-test --record-only`. Fallback resolves sha; branch lands NULL.

5. **Hard error path.** Run `endless worktree land E-XXXX --record-only` for a task with no `task/XXXX-*` branch and no `^E-XXXX:` commits on `main`. Expect the hard-error message naming what was searched.

6. **Dry-run preview.** Run `--record-only --dry-run` against a clean candidate. Expect the planned values printed, no event written, no row created.

7. **Flag-without-mode refusal.** Run `endless worktree land E-XXXX --sha abc` (no `--record-only`). Expect the hard-error message that the overrides only apply in record-only mode.

8. **Session-id lookup.** Run `--record-only --session-id 461` (int PK) and `--record-only --session-id b51f98af-bae6-4529-a6bd-48d6497d43e2` (UUID). Both should resolve to the same row's `session_id`. Pass a non-existent value; expect hard error.

Automated:
- New `tests/test_worktree_land_record_only.py` covering the cascade branches, the duplicate guard, the flag-without-mode refusal, and the session-id parser.
- Regression in `tests/test_event_bridge.py` that `emit_event` with no `ts` parameter still produces an event with envelope ts from `clock.Now()`.
- Go tests for `endless-event`'s `--ts` flag: present → uses supplied value; absent → uses `clock.Now()`; malformed → error.

`just test` (Python) and `just test-go` (Go) both green before flipping to `verify`.

## Closing

- Status to `verify` when implementation is done; include the E-1402 backfill command in the verify handoff so Mike can run it as part of confirming.
- Do not auto-land. Ask Mike.

payload.title: Enforce write-once nullable invariants via BEFORE UPDATE triggers, not CHECK constraints
payload.description: Write-once invariants for nullable columns are enforced at the DB level via BEFORE UPDATE triggers (RAISE(ABORT, ...) when OLD is non-NULL and NEW differs). CHECK constraints remain prohibited project-wide per E-892's catastrophic-data-loss lesson: SQLite cannot ALTER/DROP them without rebuilding the table. Triggers do not share that limitation; they can be DROP TRIGGERed cleanly. Application-layer pre-checks are additive for friendly error messages; the trigger is the authoritative gate.

fields.text:
# Context

Sessions last for days. `sessions.active_task_id` is the only session→task pointer today, and both `endless task claim` and `endless task bind` overwrite it. When a session pivots to a follow-up (a `cleans_up` child filed mid-stream, an in-session-discovered fix), the origin task is lost from the tmux status bar and from any cold-return orientation. Returning to a multi-day session and seeing `[E-NNNN]` in the status while the conversation is about a follow-up is exactly the failure mode this addresses.

Reading back through the conversation log as the recall mechanism is unworkable in practice — too slow, too exhausting. The status bar needs to carry both pieces of state.

# Decisions

## Schema

- **New column**: `sessions.goal_task_id INTEGER`, nullable, foreign key to `tasks(id)` ON DELETE SET NULL.
- **Write-once invariant**: once `goal_task_id` is non-NULL for a session, it cannot be changed to a different non-NULL value. Idempotent re-set with the same value is allowed.
- **Enforcement via BEFORE UPDATE trigger** (decision E-1495):

  ```sql
  CREATE TRIGGER sessions_goal_task_id_write_once
  BEFORE UPDATE OF goal_task_id ON sessions
  WHEN OLD.goal_task_id IS NOT NULL
   AND NEW.goal_task_id IS NOT OLD.goal_task_id
  BEGIN
      SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'goal_task_id is write-once; cannot change once set');
  END;
  ```

  Allows NULL → any (first write) and idempotent re-set (NEW IS OLD); refuses any change-after-set. CHECK constraints are NOT used — prohibited project-wide per E-892. Application-layer pre-checks (see `bind --include-goal` below) are additive for friendly error messages; the trigger is the authoritative gate.

- **The three existing `sessions` fields `active_goal_id`, `primary_focus_id`, `current_focus_id` are out of scope** for E-1483. They belong to a stalled task-set Mike intends to re-imagine and must not be touched.

## CLI semantics

- **`endless task claim <id>`** writes both `active_task_id` and (if currently NULL) `goal_task_id` to `<id>` for the resolved session.
- **`endless task bind <id>`** writes only `active_task_id` to `<id>`; `goal_task_id` untouched.
- **`endless task bind <id> --include-goal`** writes `active_task_id` always; writes `goal_task_id` only if it is currently NULL. If `goal_task_id` is already non-NULL, bind refuses with hard error (exit 1) and prints: `goal already set, leaving as E-NNNN` (substituting the existing goal's task id). The pre-check is for the user-facing message; the trigger remains the authoritative gate.
- **`endless task release [<id>]`** clears `active_task_id` only; `goal_task_id` is untouched. A released session retains its origin pointer.

## Events

- **New event kind: `session.goal_set`**. Entity = `session`, payload = `{ "goal_task_id": <task_id> }`. Emitted alongside `task.claimed` from `claim_item` (when goal was previously NULL) and from `bind_item` with `--include-goal` (when goal was previously NULL). Never emitted on plain `bind` or on a same-value idempotent claim.
- The existing `task.claimed` event payload is **not** modified; the goal-set side effect is carried by the new event kind so that `bind --include-goal` has the surface it needs without overloading task.claimed.
- **Projector handler** for `session.goal_set` runs `UPDATE sessions SET goal_task_id = ? WHERE id = ?`. The BEFORE UPDATE trigger enforces the invariant; the handler does not pre-check.

## tmux status-line rendering

The status renderer reads `sessions.goal_task_id` and `sessions.active_task_id` and branches:

- `goal_task_id IS NULL` OR `goal_task_id = active_task_id` → render `[E-NNNN]` (today's behavior, single value, no divergence).
- `goal_task_id != active_task_id` → render `[E-GGGG/E-AAAA]`.

Colors:

- **Active** (E-AAAA): same color the renderer currently uses for `active_task_id` (the existing "bright yellow"). Look up the exact escape from the existing renderer; do not invent a new one.
- **Goal** (E-GGGG, in the diverged form): the same color the tmux integration uses for **unselected window tabs**. Look up the existing escape from the tmux config writer or the renderer; do not invent. The intent is "background context that you can see but does not pull focus."

If either color escape cannot be located in current source, surface that as a question before implementing rather than guessing.

# Files to modify

- `internal/schema/schema.sql` — add `goal_task_id INTEGER REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE SET NULL` to `sessions`; add the BEFORE UPDATE trigger above.
- `internal/events/payload.go` — define `SessionGoalSetPayload { GoalTaskID int64 }`; add `events.KindSessionGoalSet` constant.
- `internal/events/projector.go` — add handler for `session.goal_set` that UPDATEs `sessions.goal_task_id`.
- `cmd/endless-event/main.go` / `internal/events/validate.go` — register the new event kind in the validator allow-list.
- `src/endless/task_cmd.py` — `claim_item` emits `session.goal_set` alongside `task.claimed` when the session's `goal_task_id` is currently NULL. `bind_item` accepts a new `include_goal` parameter; when True and `goal_task_id` is currently non-NULL, refuse with the `goal already set` message. When True and currently NULL, emit `session.goal_set`. When False, no goal-set event.
- `src/endless/cli.py` — `task bind` Click command gains `--include-goal` flag, threads through to `bind_item`.
- The tmux status-line renderer (likely under `cmd/endless-tmux/` or `internal/tmux/` — locate during implementation) — add the diverged-display branch reading `goal_task_id`.
- Tests below.

# Tests

Python:
- Extend `tests/test_task_claim_worktree.py`: after `claim` on a session with NULL goal, both `active_task_id` and `goal_task_id` equal the task id; after a second `claim` on a different task in the same session, `active_task_id` advances but `goal_task_id` stays at the original.
- New `tests/test_task_bind_include_goal.py`:
  - `bind --include-goal` on a session with NULL goal: both columns set; `session.goal_set` event emitted.
  - `bind --include-goal` on a session with non-NULL goal: refuses with `goal already set, leaving as E-NNNN`; no event emitted; columns unchanged.
  - Plain `bind` on a session with NULL goal: active changes; goal stays NULL; no `session.goal_set` event.
- Extend `tests/test_task_release.py`: release clears `active_task_id`, leaves `goal_task_id` intact.

Go:
- Projector test for `session.goal_set`: handler runs the UPDATE; affected-rows reflected; trigger fires on an attempted second-write-with-different-value (test the trigger directly with a raw UPDATE, expect SQLite error).
- Status-line renderer test: same / diverged / NULL-goal cases all produce the expected output strings with the right ANSI escapes.

`just test` (Python) and `just test-go` (Go) both green before flipping to `verify`.

# Out of scope

- The existing `active_goal_id`, `primary_focus_id`, `current_focus_id` columns on `sessions` (stalled task-set; not to be touched).
- Backfilling `goal_task_id` for existing session rows. New sessions get it populated on first claim; old sessions stay NULL.
- Migration logic for shipped installs (endless has one user, no shipped product).
- Mutability of `goal_task_id` via any non-claim/bind path. The trigger covers it.

# Verification

End-to-end manual checks after implementation lands:

1. **Fresh-session claim**: spawn a new session, `endless task claim E-XXXX`. Expect tmux status `[E-XXXX]`; `sessions.goal_task_id = sessions.active_task_id = XXXX`.
2. **In-session pivot via bind**: in the session from (1), `endless task bind E-YYYY`. Expect status `[E-XXXX/E-YYYY]` (goal in unselected-tab gray, active in bright yellow); `goal_task_id` unchanged, `active_task_id = YYYY`.
3. **Late goal-set**: session started without claim (NULL goal), `endless task bind E-ZZZZ --include-goal`. Expect both columns = ZZZZ; status `[E-ZZZZ]`.
4. **Write-once refusal**: in the session from (1), `endless task bind E-WWWW --include-goal`. Expect refusal with `goal already set, leaving as E-XXXX`; columns unchanged.
5. **Release preserves goal**: in the session from (1), `endless task release`. Expect `active_task_id` cleared, `goal_task_id` still XXXX, status reverts to whatever the renderer shows for "no active task" (existing behavior).
6. **Trigger is authoritative**: from `endless sql --write`, attempt `UPDATE sessions SET goal_task_id = 9999 WHERE id = <session>` on a session whose goal is set. Expect SQLite error from the trigger; row unchanged. Validates that the gate works even if app-layer is bypassed.

# Closeout

- Set status to `verify` when implementation is done and all tests pass. Include the six verification checks in the handoff so Mike can run them as part of confirming.
- Do not auto-land. Ask Mike.

# Context

Sessions last for days. `sessions.active_task_id` is the only session→task pointer today, and both `endless task claim` and `endless task bind` overwrite it. When a session pivots to a follow-up (a `cleans_up` child filed mid-stream, an in-session-discovered fix), the origin task is lost from the tmux status bar and from any cold-return orientation. Returning to a multi-day session and seeing `[E-NNNN]` in the status while the conversation is about a follow-up is exactly the failure mode this addresses.

Reading back through the conversation log as the recall mechanism is unworkable in practice — too slow, too exhausting. The status bar needs to carry both pieces of state.

# Implementation language — Go-only for DB access

**All DB access (reads AND writes) for this work happens in Go.** The Python side does only event emission and Click wiring. Do not add any `db.query(...)` or `db.execute(...)` against the `sessions` table from Python. This is the destination state E-1486 (umbrella for "Python has zero SQLite knowledge") is moving the project toward; E-1483 is blocked by E-1486 so the necessary Go-side infrastructure — session read helpers and projector write paths — is in place before E-1483 lands.

Concretely:

- **Writes**: the projector handler for `session.goal_set` (Go) is the only write path to `sessions.goal_task_id`. The BEFORE UPDATE trigger (also Go-side, since schema is owned by Go per E-1472) is the authoritative gate.
- **Reads** (e.g. the `bind --include-goal` pre-check that needs to know whether `goal_task_id` is currently NULL): call whichever Go read subcommand E-1486 ships for session lookup. Do not introduce a Python read path against `sessions` even temporarily. If the right Go subcommand does not yet exist when E-1483 is picked up, flag it as a missing E-1486 sub-deliverable rather than working around it in Python.

# Decisions

## Schema

- **New column**: `sessions.goal_task_id INTEGER`, nullable, foreign key to `tasks(id)` ON DELETE SET NULL.
- **Write-once invariant**: once `goal_task_id` is non-NULL for a session, it cannot be changed to a different non-NULL value. Idempotent re-set with the same value is allowed.
- **Enforcement via BEFORE UPDATE trigger** (decision E-1495):

  ```sql
  CREATE TRIGGER sessions_goal_task_id_write_once
  BEFORE UPDATE OF goal_task_id ON sessions
  WHEN OLD.goal_task_id IS NOT NULL
   AND NEW.goal_task_id IS NOT OLD.goal_task_id
  BEGIN
      SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'goal_task_id is write-once; cannot change once set');
  END;
  ```

  Allows NULL → any (first write) and idempotent re-set (NEW IS OLD); refuses any change-after-set. CHECK constraints are NOT used — prohibited project-wide per E-892. Application-layer pre-checks (see `bind --include-goal` below) are additive for friendly error messages; the trigger is the authoritative gate.

- **The three existing `sessions` fields `active_goal_id`, `primary_focus_id`, `current_focus_id` are out of scope** for E-1483. They belong to a stalled task-set Mike intends to re-imagine and must not be touched.

## CLI semantics

- **`endless task claim <id>`** writes both `active_task_id` and (if currently NULL) `goal_task_id` to `<id>` for the resolved session.
- **`endless task bind <id>`** writes only `active_task_id` to `<id>`; `goal_task_id` untouched.
- **`endless task bind <id> --include-goal`** writes `active_task_id` always; writes `goal_task_id` only if it is currently NULL. If `goal_task_id` is already non-NULL, bind refuses with hard error (exit 1) and prints: `goal already set, leaving as E-NNNN` (substituting the existing goal's task id). The pre-check is for the user-facing message; the trigger remains the authoritative gate.
- **`endless task release [<id>]`** clears `active_task_id` only; `goal_task_id` is untouched. A released session retains its origin pointer.

## Events

- **New event kind: `session.goal_set`**. Entity = `session`, payload = `{ "goal_task_id": <task_id> }`. Emitted alongside `task.claimed` from `claim_item` (when goal was previously NULL) and from `bind_item` with `--include-goal` (when goal was previously NULL). Never emitted on plain `bind` or on a same-value idempotent claim.
- The existing `task.claimed` event payload is **not** modified; the goal-set side effect is carried by the new event kind so that `bind --include-goal` has the surface it needs without overloading task.claimed.
- **Projector handler** for `session.goal_set` runs `UPDATE sessions SET goal_task_id = ? WHERE id = ?`. The BEFORE UPDATE trigger enforces the invariant; the handler does not pre-check.

## tmux status-line rendering

The status renderer reads `sessions.goal_task_id` and `sessions.active_task_id` and branches:

- `goal_task_id IS NULL` OR `goal_task_id = active_task_id` → render `[E-NNNN]` (today's behavior, single value, no divergence).
- `goal_task_id != active_task_id` → render `[E-GGGG/E-AAAA]`.

Colors:

- **Active** (E-AAAA): same color the renderer currently uses for `active_task_id` (the existing "bright yellow"). Look up the exact escape from the existing renderer; do not invent a new one.
- **Goal** (E-GGGG, in the diverged form): the same color the tmux integration uses for **unselected window tabs**. Look up the existing escape from the tmux config writer or the renderer; do not invent. The intent is "background context that you can see but does not pull focus."

If either color escape cannot be located in current source, surface that as a question before implementing rather than guessing.

# Files to modify

- `internal/schema/schema.sql` — add `goal_task_id INTEGER REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE SET NULL` to `sessions`; add the BEFORE UPDATE trigger above.
- `internal/events/payload.go` — define `SessionGoalSetPayload { GoalTaskID int64 }`; add `events.KindSessionGoalSet` constant.
- `internal/events/projector.go` — add handler for `session.goal_set` that UPDATEs `sessions.goal_task_id`.
- `cmd/endless-event/main.go` / `internal/events/validate.go` — register the new event kind in the validator allow-list.
- `src/endless/task_cmd.py` — Python event emission and CLI wiring only; **no SQL** (per Implementation-language section above). `claim_item` emits `session.goal_set` alongside `task.claimed` when the session's `goal_task_id` is currently NULL (NULL state read via Go subcommand). `bind_item` accepts a new `include_goal` parameter; when True, reads current `goal_task_id` via Go subcommand. Non-NULL → refuse with the `goal already set, leaving as E-NNNN` message, emit nothing. NULL → emit `session.goal_set`. When False, no goal-set event.
- `src/endless/cli.py` — `task bind` Click command gains `--include-goal` flag, threads through to `bind_item`.
- The tmux status-line renderer (likely under `cmd/endless-tmux/` or `internal/tmux/` — locate during implementation) — add the diverged-display branch reading `goal_task_id`.
- Tests below.

# Tests

Python:
- Extend `tests/test_task_claim_worktree.py`: after `claim` on a session with NULL goal, both `active_task_id` and `goal_task_id` equal the task id; after a second `claim` on a different task in the same session, `active_task_id` advances but `goal_task_id` stays at the original.
- New `tests/test_task_bind_include_goal.py`:
  - `bind --include-goal` on a session with NULL goal: both columns set; `session.goal_set` event emitted.
  - `bind --include-goal` on a session with non-NULL goal: refuses with `goal already set, leaving as E-NNNN`; no event emitted; columns unchanged.
  - Plain `bind` on a session with NULL goal: active changes; goal stays NULL; no `session.goal_set` event.
- Extend `tests/test_task_release.py`: release clears `active_task_id`, leaves `goal_task_id` intact.

Go:
- Projector test for `session.goal_set`: handler runs the UPDATE; affected-rows reflected; trigger fires on an attempted second-write-with-different-value (test the trigger directly with a raw UPDATE, expect SQLite error).
- Status-line renderer test: same / diverged / NULL-goal cases all produce the expected output strings with the right ANSI escapes.

`just test` (Python) and `just test-go` (Go) both green before flipping to `verify`.

# Out of scope

- The existing `active_goal_id`, `primary_focus_id`, `current_focus_id` columns on `sessions` (stalled task-set; not to be touched).
- Backfilling `goal_task_id` for existing session rows. New sessions get it populated on first claim; old sessions stay NULL.
- Migration logic for shipped installs (endless has one user, no shipped product).
- Mutability of `goal_task_id` via any non-claim/bind path. The trigger covers it.

# Verification

End-to-end manual checks after implementation lands:

1. **Fresh-session claim**: spawn a new session, `endless task claim E-XXXX`. Expect tmux status `[E-XXXX]`; `sessions.goal_task_id = sessions.active_task_id = XXXX`.
2. **In-session pivot via bind**: in the session from (1), `endless task bind E-YYYY`. Expect status `[E-XXXX/E-YYYY]` (goal in unselected-tab gray, active in bright yellow); `goal_task_id` unchanged, `active_task_id = YYYY`.
3. **Late goal-set**: session started without claim (NULL goal), `endless task bind E-ZZZZ --include-goal`. Expect both columns = ZZZZ; status `[E-ZZZZ]`.
4. **Write-once refusal**: in the session from (1), `endless task bind E-WWWW --include-goal`. Expect refusal with `goal already set, leaving as E-XXXX`; columns unchanged.
5. **Release preserves goal**: in the session from (1), `endless task release`. Expect `active_task_id` cleared, `goal_task_id` still XXXX, status reverts to whatever the renderer shows for "no active task" (existing behavior).
6. **Trigger is authoritative**: from `endless sql --write`, attempt `UPDATE sessions SET goal_task_id = 9999 WHERE id = <session>` on a session whose goal is set. Expect SQLite error from the trigger; row unchanged. Validates that the gate works even if app-layer is bypassed.

# Closeout

- Set status to `verify` when implementation is done and all tests pass. Include the six verification checks in the handoff so Mike can run them as part of confirming.
- Do not auto-land. Ask Mike.

fields.text:
# E-1479 — `--record-only` flag for `endless worktree land`

## Context

`task_landings` projects `task.landed` events emitted by `endless worktree land`. Lands that happened outside that flow — manual `git merge`, or before the table existed (E-1402, landed 2026-05-17) — have no sanctioned way to be registered after the fact; a direct INSERT would bypass the event-sourced contract. `--record-only` lets the existing `land` verb emit a `task.landed` event for an already-completed land while skipping all rebase / dedup / orphan-drop / ff-merge work. First use case: backfill the missing `task_landings` row for E-1402.

Flag (not a sibling `record-landing` verb): two-segment verbs are a CLI smell here, and a flag is more discoverable via `land --help`.

### Why `task_landings.branch` stays (with a fix)

Confirmed `branch` lives on `task_landings`, not `sessions`. Its **only** non-test consumer is the worktree reaper (`internal/monitor/reap_worktrees.go:115-154`): it reads the most-recent landing's `branch` and runs `git branch -D <branch>` during TTL cleanup (introduced by E-1337, commit `832b7aa`). So `branch` is load-bearing for a **live** land, but worthless for a record-only of an already-reaped branch (E-1402) — nothing to clean up. NULL is the correct value there. The column is `TEXT NOT NULL` today (written against instruction); it must accept NULL.

## Sequencing — HELD until E-1486 lands

Per decision: the record-only DB reads go in **Go**, not Python `db.query`, and E-1479 waits for **E-1486** (the Python→Go DB-access port, in flight in another session) so its Go reads build on that layer. Do not implement now. E-1479 is set `blocked` with a `blocked_by` dependency on E-1486. The Go DB API specifics are deferred until E-1486 exists — this plan fixes *what* reads are needed, not their exact Go signatures.

## Design

### Auto-discovery (record-only, queried against the **main** checkout)

`branch`/`sha` cascade:
1. **Primary** — `git for-each-ref --format='%(refname:short) %(objectname)' refs/heads/task/<id>-*`, drop tuples whose tip is already in `task_landings` for this task. Exactly one left → use its branch + tip sha. Multiple → refuse, listing candidates. Zero → fall through.
2. **Fallback** — `git log <base> --grep '^E-<id>:' --format='%H %cI'`, drop already-recorded shas, pick the newest remaining as `sha`; `branch` is left empty → NULL (a deleted branch name is unrecoverable: commits don't store it, ff-only merges leave no merge-commit message, the ref's reflog is gone).
3. **Hard error** — neither path yields a candidate; message names what was searched.

`--sha` supplied → skip the cascade; `branch` is empty → NULL unless `--branch` given (the E-1402 path).
`landed_at` ← chosen commit's `%cI` (overridable by `--at`). `session_id` ← resolver/current session (overridable by `--session-id`).

`branch` is recorded only when the ref is still alive (sole benefit: later reaper cleanup); this rides for free on the SHA discovery the cascade already does.

### Flags

- `--record-only` — bare flag, enables the mode.
- `--sha <hash>` — override sha; skips cascade.
- `--branch <name>` — override branch; `--branch ''` records NULL deliberately.
- `--at <iso>` — override `landed_at`. Parse with `datetime.fromisoformat(at.replace('Z','+00:00'))` (matches `session_cmd.py:976`); normalize to offset-bearing (naive → `.astimezone()` for local offset) before handing to the Go binary.
- `--session-id <id-or-uuid>` — override resolver; integer → `sessions.id`, else exact/prefix `sessions.session_id` (logic mirrors `_resolve_session`, session_cmd.py:61, reimplemented as a Go read post-E-1486). Miss → hard error naming the value.
- Any of `--sha/--branch/--at/--session-id` without `--record-only` → hard error.
- `--record-only --dry-run` → print planned (task_id, branch, sha, landed_at, session_id, and auto-discovered/override per field); emit nothing.

### Branch nullability + reaper fix

- `internal/schema/schema.sql` — `branch TEXT NOT NULL` → `branch TEXT` (fresh builds).
- `internal/schema/changes/e-1479-task-landings-branch-nullable.sql` (new) — rebuild live table to nullable `branch`: CREATE `task_landings_new` (same cols/FKs, `branch TEXT`), `INSERT … SELECT *`, DROP, RENAME, recreate `idx_task_landings_task`. Mirrors `e-1459-reshape-schema-version.sql`; runs in the dispatcher's `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` (nothing references `task_landings`, so FK-safe). Auto-applied at land time by the `just land` recipe (Justfile:134) before the ff-merge.
- `internal/events/projector.go` `replayTaskLanded` (and `executor.go execTaskLanded`) — insert SQL NULL when payload `branch` is empty (`var b any = p.Branch; if p.Branch=="" { b = nil }`). Normal lands always pass a real branch → unchanged.
- `internal/monitor/reap_worktrees.go:115-154` — **required by the above**: scan `branch` into `sql.NullString`; only `git branch -D` when `branch.Valid && branch.String != ""`. A NULL into a plain `string` errors and aborts the reap.

### `landed_at` is UTC; `--at`/commit-date mechanism

`evt.TS` is kairos base32, not raw ISO: `Event.Validate()` runs `kairos.Parse(e.TS)` and the projector renders `landed_at` via `kairosToISO(evt.TS) = …Physical().UTC().Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05")` (projector.go:418). So an override is a kairos timestamp and `landed_at` is always UTC (consistent with every existing row — confirmed decision).

- `cmd/endless-event/main.go` — `runEmit`/`run` gain `--ts <iso>`; when set, `time.Parse(time.RFC3339, …)` → `kairos.New(parsed, 0, nid)` (timestamp.go:26) replaces `clock.Now()`. Downstream (envelope `ts.String()`, validation, projector) unchanged. Malformed → error.
- `src/endless/event_bridge.py` — `emit_event` gains `ts: str|None=None`; when set, appends `--ts <ts>`. Default path byte-for-byte unchanged.
- Record-only path passes offset-bearing ISO for `ts` (auto: `%cI`; `--at`: normalized as above).

Consequence: E-1402's `2026-05-17T22:48:59-04:00` lands as `landed_at = 2026-05-18T02:48:59`.

### Idempotency

Refuse on duplicate `(task_id, sha)` in `task_landings`; error names the existing row's `id` + `landed_at`. Re-landings (advanced sha) still work.

### Success output (one physical line for copy-paste)

```
• Recorded landing of E-NNNN (branch <name-or-(null)>, sha <short>) at <landed_at>
```

### Help text

`--record-only`: "Register a task.landed event for a land that already happened; skip rebase/ff-merge. Auto-discovers branch/sha/landed_at from local git; override with --branch/--sha/--at if needed."

## Files to modify

- `src/endless/cli.py` — `worktree_land` gains the five flags + flag-without-mode validation.
- `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py` — early `record_only` branch in `land_worktree` needing no on-disk worktree: auto-discovery, dedup guard, session-id resolution, dry-run; `_record_landing` extended with optional `ts` + empty-branch-as-NULL (or sibling helper). Normal flow unchanged.
- `src/endless/event_bridge.py` — `emit_event` `ts` passthrough.
- `cmd/endless-event/main.go` — `--ts` flag.
- `internal/schema/schema.sql` — `branch` nullable.
- `internal/schema/changes/e-1479-task-landings-branch-nullable.sql` (new) — live-table rebuild.
- `internal/events/projector.go`, `internal/events/executor.go` — empty branch → NULL insert.
- `internal/monitor/reap_worktrees.go` — NULL-safe branch read.
- **Go DB reads (post-E-1486):** dedup guard, auto-discovery "already-recorded sha" filter, `--session-id` resolution — implemented against E-1486's Go DB layer.
- `tests/test_worktree_land_record_only.py` (new); `tests/test_event_bridge.py` (`ts` passthrough regression); Go tests for `endless-event --ts` and the projector empty-branch→NULL + reaper NULL-branch path.

## Out of scope

- Backfilling historical rows beyond E-1402; touching the normal land flow; restructuring `task_landings` beyond branch nullability; `--record-only` on other commands.

## Verification (from the main checkout after E-1479 lands; `just land E-1479` auto-applies the schema change)

1. **E-1402 backfill:** `endless worktree land E-1402 --record-only --sha 33a2ea48ef1a2cfefdd9d5d86e1eef5a410434b5`, then `endless sql "SELECT * FROM task_landings WHERE task_id = 1402"` → one row: that sha, `branch IS NULL`, `landed_at = 2026-05-18T02:48:59`, `session_id` = the confirming session.
2. **Schema:** `PRAGMA table_info(task_landings)` → `branch` `notnull = 0`.
3. **Reaper NULL-safe:** a NULL-branch landing whose worktree dir still exists is reaped without error (worktree removed, branch deletion skipped).
4. **Live land + retry:** land a test task normally; re-run `--record-only` without `--sha` → duplicate-guard refusal.
5. **Auto-discovery happy path / fallback / hard error / dry-run / flag-without-mode / session-id lookup** — per the flag matrix above.
6. `just test` (Python) + `just test-go` (Go) green before `verify`.

## Closing

- This session does **not** implement. E-1479 is set `blocked` with a `blocked_by` dependency on E-1486.
- When E-1486 lands, a follow-up session implements against the Go DB layer, then flips to `verify` with the E-1402 backfill command in the handoff. Do not auto-land.

fields.analysis: Session-next accuracy finding (session 504 investigation, 2026-05-27): session_tasks records ANY task touched under a session_id, agent or human, including incidental grooming. Example: E-999/E-1064/E-1073 were recorded against session 504 because that session (the agent, acting on Mike's behalf) ran task-update on them. Accurate history, but not 'what the session worked on'. So the relation column must let session-next separate goal/surfaced/revisited work from incidental edits, and the render-side terminal-status filter (per E-1421) must drop completed/confirmed touches from the Remaining view. Actor-identity gap: actor.id is currently USER@hostname (event_bridge.py emit_event, ~line 68) for BOTH agent- and human-run CLI, so they are indistinguishable at the actor level. Direction (per Mike): set actor_id to 'claude' vs 'user', detected via a Claude-Code execution-context env var (e.g. CLAUDECODE), instead of a hardcoded username. Needed for multi-user (future scope) and lets the relation classifier weight agent vs human touches. Caveat: this alone would NOT exclude E-999/1064/1073 (those were agent grooming, so id would be 'claude'); the relation column plus the terminal-status filter are what handle incidental-vs-goal.

Session-next accuracy finding (session 504 investigation, 2026-05-27): session_tasks records ANY task touched under a session_id, agent or human, including incidental grooming. Example: E-999/E-1064/E-1073 were recorded against session 504 because that session (the agent, acting on Mike's behalf) ran task-update on them. Accurate history, but not 'what the session worked on'. So the relation column must let session-next separate goal/surfaced/revisited work from incidental edits, and the render-side terminal-status filter (per E-1421) must drop completed/confirmed touches from the Remaining view.

Actor-identity (OPEN QUESTION -- discuss when working E-1462; not yet decided, do not pre-frame as multiple-choice): actor.id is currently built as USER@hostname (event_bridge.py emit_event, ~line 68), e.g. mikeschinkel@m4macmini.local, and is identical for agent- and human-run CLI, so the two are indistinguishable at the actor level. The current user@machine form carries useful provenance (who AND which machine) worth preserving -- do NOT collapse it to a bare 'claude'/'user'. Candidate to discuss: keep user@machine but prefix it with a role, e.g. agent:claude@m4macmini.local vs user:mikeschinkel@m4macmini.local. That distinguishes agent from human, keeps machine provenance, and generalizes to multi-user (future scope). Open sub-questions: the role vocabulary (agent/user vs claude/human), how agent context is detected (e.g. the CLAUDECODE env var Claude Code injects), and whether the role lives in actor.id or a separate actor field. Caveat: distinguishing agent from human filters human-CLI pollution but would NOT exclude E-999/1064/1073 (agent grooming, so role would be 'agent'); the relation column plus terminal-status filter are what handle incidental-vs-goal.

fields.text:
# E-1485 — Surface closeable parent tasks in the per-project next list

## Decided (Mike, 2026-05-27)

The curated next list (E-1421) is session-authored, so closeable parents must be
**written into the list as pending items**, NOT computed at render time — a
curating session has to see them to pull them into a ranked lane.

- **Target:** the persistent **`project_next_pending`** table (schema.sql:330) —
  separate from `project_next_lanes`/`project_next_items`, so it **survives a
  `revise`** (which only rewrites lanes/items). This is the "uncategorized /
  pending" bucket. Dedup via `UNIQUE(project_next_id, task_id)` → `INSERT OR IGNORE`.
- **"Closeable parent"** = a task whose children are ALL terminal
  (confirmed / assumed / declined / obsolete) while the parent itself is NON-terminal.

## Ground truth / scope

`project_next_pending` exists in the schema but has **no production writer** today
(only a test inserts). The E-1421-design "auto-add urgent" was never built. So
E-1485 is the **first writer** of this table and defines the add-to-pending path
that a later urgent-auto-add can reuse.

## Mechanism

Maintain `project_next_pending` as a derived projection of task state:
- When a task changes to a terminal status, re-evaluate its parent: if all the
  parent's children are terminal and the parent is non-terminal →
  `INSERT OR IGNORE` the parent into `project_next_pending`
  (reason e.g. "all children terminal", `added_at = now`).
- Remove the parent from pending when it's no longer closeable (a new non-terminal
  child appears / a child reopens) or when the parent reaches a terminal status.
- Render: `endless task next` shows pending items after the ranked lanes. If a task
  is already in a curated lane, don't also show it in pending. (Verify whether
  `task next` already reads `project_next_pending`; wire it if not.)

## Open fork (resolve before flipping to ready)

Where closeability detection runs:
- (a) **Go event-projector reaction** to `task.fields_updated` (status) events —
  race-free, event-sourced, no new infra. RECOMMENDED.
- (b) **endless-hook prompt-hook scan** on session events — matches the original
  "via Claude and Prompt hooks" framing, but coarser/periodic.

## Clearing semantics to settle at plan time

Parent → `verify` is NON-terminal, so a closeable parent stays closeable and would
linger in pending until confirmed. Decide: does the user setting the parent to
`verify` clear it from pending, or only a terminal status does?

## Relations

relates_to E-1421 (the curated next list this extends).

payload.title: Make spawn/claim tolerate an orphan task branch instead of failing
payload.description: When a task's branch (task/<id>-<slug>) exists but its worktree directory is gone (left behind by 'worktree drop' or the reaper, which both keep the branch), create_task_worktree fails on 'git worktree add -b' with 'a branch named X already exists', blocking spawn and claim. Recover instead: if the branch's only delta from main is the plan file, recreate the worktree fresh from main and re-materialize the plan from tasks.text (reconciling DB-vs-file disagreements); if the branch carries real (non-plan) work, error with actionable guidance. Every error must name the problem and hand the user a command or two to resolve it.

payload.title: Tighten spawn ownership gate: consult goal_task_id, emit switch command, handle dead-pane owners
payload.description: spawn's _check_task_ownership (task_cmd.py) only consults active_task_id, names the owning tmux pane without a paste-ready switch command, and silently reclaims dead-pane owners. After sessions.goal_task_id lands (E-1483): also refuse when a live session holds the task as its goal; for a live owner emit a runnable 'tmux select-window -t <pane>' line instead of just naming the pane; and for a dead-pane owner proceed with the spawn (you cannot switch to a dead session) but print a loud notice naming the dead session and the 'endless task release E-NNN' command to clear its stale pointer.

payload.title: Add agent-facing guide cross-reference and per-command --help augmentation

fields.text:
# Agent-facing guide cross-reference + per-command `--help` augmentation

## Context

An agent told to "read the guide and update the spawn command" ran `endless guide`,
saw the index, then ran `--help` on a dozen commands and **never read a guide section** —
even though the index already says orchestration covers "spawning sessions."

Two distinct failures:

1. **Reverse-lookup gap.** The index is keyed *by section* ("orchestration covers A, B, C").
   An agent arrives keyed *by command/topic* ("I have `spawn` — where do I go?") and must
   read all five prose blurbs and infer the mapping. There is no scannable
   `command/topic → section` table.
2. **The `--help` instinct beats the guide.** Even with the blurb present, the agent trusted
   `--help` over the guide. The fix must intercept *at* `--help`: the agent reached for it, so
   put the signpost there.

This change adds (1) a generated `command/topic → section` cross-reference in `index.md`, and
(2) per-command `--help` augmentation that, for agents, prepends a directive pointing at the
right guide section. Both are fed by the same per-command map files, regenerated by an
LLM-driven slash command because deciding "which section explains command X" is semantic and
cannot be done well in pure code.

## Converged decisions (settled with Mike)

- **Cross-reference lives in `index.md`** (not a separate section — a separate appendix has a
  chicken-and-egg problem). Maps **commands *and* topics**, each to a section + a "covers" phrase.
- **`--help` augmentation:** pointer **prepended above** normal help (normal usage/flags still
  shown below), phrased as a **directive** (agents under-weight soft suggestions). An optional
  short per-command note only where the guide genuinely lacks a command-specific trap. The map
  file *points*; the guide *explains* (no duplication → no drift).
- **Agent detection** via the existing `_is_claude_code_agent()` (`CLAUDECODE=1`); a `--agent-view`
  flag forces the agent rendering so a human can see/debug it.
- **Coverage:** every command, top-level and subcommand. A command with no good section is a
  **guide gap, not a map gap** — surfaced as a coverage report that drives guide improvement.
- **Maintenance split:** code scaffolds (walk the Click tree, list sections/anchors) → LLM fills
  the semantic mapping → code validates + assembles. The LLM never enumerates commands from memory.
- **Layering:** deterministic logic in a **Python module** (graduation-ready: this *might* later
  become an end-user `endless` verb, so logic does not live in shell); `just` recipes are dev-side
  wrappers + a drift gate; the LLM step is a **project slash command** (`.claude/commands/`,
  repo-tracked, dev tooling — not a shipped verb).

## Components

1. `src/endless/guide_map.py` **(new)** — deterministic primitives, importable + testable:
   - `walk_commands(main) -> list[str]`: recursively walk `main.commands` (Click `Group`),
     collecting full command paths (e.g. `task spawn`, `worktree land`). Reuses the existing
     `endless.cli:main` object.
   - `guide_sections() -> dict[slug, list[anchor]]`: glob `docs/guide/*.md` (excluding `index`),
     parse `##`/`###` headers → GitHub-style anchor slugs.
   - `load_map(command_path) -> MapEntry | None`: read `docs/guide/help/<kebab-path>.md`.
   - `validate() -> Report`: every command has a map file with a `section:` that resolves to a
     real guide slug; `_topics.md` refs resolve; the `index.md` generated block matches a fresh
     assembly. Returns missing/dangling/stale lists.
   - `assemble_index_block() -> str`: build the `index.md` table from all map files + `_topics.md`.
   - `command_path_to_filename` / back: `task spawn` ⇄ `task-spawn.md`.

2. `src/endless/agent_help.py` **(new)** — Click help augmentation:
   - Promote `_is_claude_code_agent()` here from `task_cmd.py:226` (re-export there to avoid churn).
   - `_agent_view_requested()`: True if `--agent-view` was seen (set by the root group, below).
   - `class AgentHelpMixin` overriding `format_help(self, ctx, formatter)`: if
     `_is_claude_code_agent() or _agent_view_requested()`, prepend the agent block built from
     `load_map(ctx.command_path-without-"endless")`; then `super().format_help(...)`. Missing map
     file → for agents, a generic one-liner ("no section mapped; run `endless guide`"); never errors.
   - `class AgentAwareGroup(AgentHelpMixin, DBAwareGroup)` and
     `class AgentAwareCommand(AgentHelpMixin, click.Command)`, each setting
     `command_class = AgentAwareCommand` / `group_class = AgentAwareGroup` as class attributes so
     the whole tree inherits from one root change.

3. `src/endless/cli.py` **(edits)**:
   - Root group `cls=DBAwareGroup` (line 114) → `cls=AgentAwareGroup`.
   - Extend the argv pre-scan in `DBAwareGroup.main()` (lines 82-100) to also consume `--agent-view`
     (strip it, record it via `agent_help`) so Click's per-command `--help` doesn't reject the
     unknown flag — identical pattern to `--db`.

4. `docs/guide/help/` **(new dir, generated)** — one file per command, dependency-free header:
   ```
   section: orchestration
   covers: Spawning another Claude session — the handoff is generated, not authored

   <optional agent note: a command-specific trap not in the guide>
   ```
   - `section:` (required, comma-separated for multi-section) — validated against guide slugs.
   - `covers:` (required) — short phrase shown in the index table and the `--help` directive.
   - Body (optional) — the per-command note.
   - `_topics.md` — LLM-curated topic→section rows (topics aren't enumerable from code), same header
     shape; `_`-prefixed so command-file iteration skips it.

5. `docs/guide/index.md` **(edit)** — insert a delimited, regeneration-owned block:
   ```
   <!-- BEGIN generated: command/topic cross-reference (regenerate via /regenerate-guide) -->
   ## Where to look (command / topic → section)

   | Command / topic | Section | Covers |
   |---|---|---|
   | `endless task spawn` | orchestration | Spawning another Claude session... |
   | _topic:_ commit-to-main policy | orchestration | ... |
   <!-- END generated -->
   ```
   Only this block is rewritten; hand-authored prose is untouched.

6. `Justfile` **(edits)** — thin wrappers over `guide_map.py` (match existing recipe style):
   - `just guide-scaffold` — print the command tree + sections/anchors (the skeleton the LLM fills).
   - `just guide-index` — reassemble the `index.md` generated block from map files (deterministic).
   - `just guide-check` — run `validate()`, non-zero exit on any missing/dangling/stale (pre-land gate).

7. `.claude/commands/regenerate-guide.md` **(new, repo-tracked)** — LLM orchestrator:
   run `just guide-scaffold` → for each command without/with-stale mapping, read the relevant guide
   section and write/fix `docs/guide/help/<path>.md` (and `_topics.md`) → run `just guide-index` →
   run `just guide-check` and report remaining coverage gaps as guide-improvement candidates.

## Agent `--help` rendering (example: `endless task spawn --help`)

```
▸ AGENT — read this before using this command:
    endless guide orchestration   (Spawning another Claude session — the handoff is generated, not authored)
    <optional one-line note>

Usage: endless task spawn [OPTIONS] ...     <- normal Click help unchanged, below
...
```

Humans (no `CLAUDECODE`, no `--agent-view`) see only the normal Click help.

## Verification

1. `just build` (or editable install already live). Run from the worktree.
2. Coverage gate: `just guide-check` exits non-zero and lists any unmapped command — add a map
   file for each until it passes. Then `just guide-check` is green.
3. Agent view: `CLAUDECODE=1 endless task spawn --help` shows the directive block above usage;
   `endless task spawn --help --agent-view` (no `CLAUDECODE`) shows the same; plain
   `endless task spawn --help` shows only normal help.
4. Drift gate: rename/add a `##` header in a guide section, run `just guide-check` → it flags the
   dangling/stale ref. Add a new subcommand stub → `just guide-check` flags the missing map file.
5. Index: `endless guide` prints the cross-reference table; `endless task spawn` and at least one
   topic row resolve to the right section.
6. `just guide-index` is idempotent (running twice produces no diff).
7. Tests: add `guide_map.py` unit tests (tree walk count, anchor slugging, validate() on a fixture
   with a missing file and a dangling ref). `just test`.

## Endless task steps (after plan approval — blocked under plan mode)

1. `endless task add "Add agent-facing guide cross-reference and per-command --help augmentation"
   --type task` (use the literal printed ID).
2. `endless task claim <id>` (creates the worktree; run subsequent work there).
3. `endless task update <id> --text <this plan>` (attaches plan, auto-promotes to `ready`).
4. Implement components 1–7; run the verification above.
5. `endless task update <id> --status verify` with how-to-test in the handoff; do not mark confirmed.
